## Silent Skill Cards
playbook/cards/malaise.md
@@ -11,10 +11,12 @@ Silent Skill, X cost (Exhaust).
Malaise is the Silent's strongest boss debuff. It consumes all remaining energy and applies that amount as both Weak and permanent Strength reduction. Unlike Piercing Wail (temporary -6 Str for 1 turn), Malaise's Strength reduction is permanent for the combat.
+**CRITICAL -- X-COST ENERGY TRAP:** Malaise automatically uses ALL remaining energy. You CANNOT specify X manually. If you have 4E and play Malaise first, X=4 and you have 0E left for block cards. This has directly caused a death: player intended Malaise X=1 but it consumed all 4E, leaving 0E for Defend/Survivor, taking 21 unblocked damage at 19 HP. **To control X: play block cards and other spells FIRST, then play Malaise LAST with only the desired energy remaining.** See mechanics.md "X-Cost Cards" section.
+
## Optimal Usage
- **Save for boss fights.** Malaise exhausts -- single use per combat. Do not waste on hallway fights or elites unless the fight is genuinely lethal.
-- **Play LAST on your turn** after all other non-X-cost cards. Malaise consumes all remaining energy. Play block cards, Powers, and 0-cost attacks first, then Malaise with remaining E.
+- **Play LAST on your turn** after all other non-X-cost cards. Malaise consumes ALL remaining energy automatically. Play block cards, Powers, and 0-cost attacks first, then Malaise with the desired remaining E. There is no way to specify X -- X equals your remaining energy when Malaise is played.
- **Maximum value at high energy.** With 5E and Malaise as the only play: X=5 gives -5 Str and 5 Weak. Combined, this reduces enemy damage by ~50-70% for multiple turns.
- **Do NOT use for Artifact stripping.** Malaise is too valuable to spend on removing 1 Artifact charge. Use Neutralize, Crippling Cloud, or other cheap debuffs for Artifact stripping.
playbook/cards/skewer.md
@@ -0,0 +1,29 @@
+# Skewer
+
+Silent Attack, X cost.
+
+**Effect:** Deal 7 damage X times to one enemy.
+
+**Base:** 7 * X damage.
+**Upgraded:** 10 * X damage.
+
+## Key Mechanic
+
+Skewer is an X-cost attack that scales with remaining energy. High energy (4-5E) produces massive single-target burst. With Vulnerable, each hit deals 10 (base) or 15 (upgraded) damage.
+
+**CRITICAL -- X-COST ENERGY TRAP:** Skewer automatically uses ALL remaining energy. You CANNOT specify X manually. If you have 4E and play Skewer first, X=4 and you have 0E left for block cards. **To control X: play block cards and other spells FIRST, then play Skewer LAST with only the desired energy remaining.** See mechanics.md "X-Cost Cards" section.
+
+## Optimal Usage
+
+- **Play LAST on your turn** after all block cards and non-X-cost attacks. Skewer consumes ALL remaining energy automatically.
+- **Best on free turns (enemy buffing/defending)** where all energy can go to damage without sacrificing block.
+- **Pairs with Energy Potions** for massive burst (5E Skewer = 35/50 damage).
+- **Pairs with Vulnerable** for 50% more damage per hit.
+
+## Observed Usage
+
+Confirmed as primary Hexaghost kill card -- Skewer at 5E (with Sozu + Energy Potion) dealt 36 Vulnerable damage in a single turn.
+
+## Draft Priority
+
+Moderate priority. Strong burst finisher for boss fights and elites. Less useful in hallway fights where energy must be split between block and damage.
playbook/cards/whirlwind.md
@@ -2,7 +2,7 @@
AOE that scales with energy. Uses ALL remaining energy.
WHEN IT'S GOOD: Turn 1 with bonus energy (Lantern, Seeing Red+) for massive AOE. Against Slime Boss split (hits both slimes). Against Sentries (45 total AOE for 3E).
-DECISION POINTS: Play LAST -- it uses all remaining energy. Plan other plays first, then dump remaining energy into Whirlwind. With Strength, each hit gains +Str damage.
+DECISION POINTS: Play LAST -- it uses ALL remaining energy automatically. You CANNOT specify X manually. X equals your remaining energy when Whirlwind is played. To control X, spend energy on other cards first, then play Whirlwind last. With Strength, each hit gains +Str damage.
UPGRADE: 5->8 per hit. Significant improvement.
SYNERGIES: Extra energy (Lantern, Seeing Red+, Berserk), Strength (each hit gains bonus).
MATCHUPS: Slime Boss split -- hits both slimes. Sentries -- 15 damage to all 3. Gremlin Leader -- clears summoned gremlins.
playbook/mechanics.md
@@ -157,7 +157,30 @@ When you play a card by numeric index, all cards after it shift down by 1. This
- Base energy: 3 per turn (Ironclad, no modifiers).
- Energy resets each turn -- unspent energy is WASTED.
- Energy modifiers: Lantern (+1E turn 1), Ancient Tea Set (+2E turn 1 after rest), Berserk (+1E/turn permanently), Seeing Red+ (+2E one time), Happy Flower (+1E every 3 turns).
-- X-cost cards (Whirlwind) use ALL remaining energy. Play LAST.
+- X-cost cards (Whirlwind, Malaise, Skewer) use ALL remaining energy. See X-Cost Cards section below.
+
+### X-Cost Cards (CRITICAL -- CONFIRMED FATAL ERROR)
+
+X-cost cards (Whirlwind, Malaise, Skewer, and others) **automatically consume ALL remaining energy when played.** You CANNOT specify X manually. X is always equal to your remaining energy at the moment the card is played.
+
+**This means:** If you have 4E and play Malaise intending X=1, the game will use X=4 and consume all 4 energy. You will have 0E remaining for block cards. This has directly caused a death.
+
+**To control X, spend energy on other cards FIRST:**
+1. Decide what X value you want.
+2. Calculate: energy to spend on other cards = current energy - desired X.
+3. Play those other cards FIRST (Defend, block cards, Powers, 0-cost attacks).
+4. Play the X-cost card LAST with only the desired energy remaining.
+
+**Example:** You have 4E and want Malaise X=1 + 2 Defends + Deflect.
+- WRONG: `play Malaise 0` (uses X=4, 0E left, Defends cannot be played)
+- CORRECT: `play Defend; play Defend; play Deflect; play Malaise 0` (3E spent on block, Malaise uses X=1)
+
+**X-cost cards in the game:**
+- **Whirlwind** (Ironclad Attack): 5 damage X times to ALL enemies
+- **Malaise** (Silent Skill, Exhaust): X Weak + X Str Down to one enemy
+- **Skewer** (Silent Attack): 7 damage X times to one enemy
+
+**RULE: ALWAYS play X-cost cards LAST on a turn.** Never play an X-cost card first unless you genuinely want to spend all energy on it.
---

High-level strategic principles for Ironclad runs.
-**CURRENT STATUS (162 runs, 5 WINS):** Three Ironclad victories (Runs 147, 148, 150). Two Silent victories (Run 158 vs Time Eater, Run 162 vs Awakened One). Twelve Silent runs total (Runs 151-162). FOUR proven winning formulas now exist: (1) Ironclad Snecko Eye + Immolate+ + Limit Break, (2) Ironclad Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam, (3) Silent Accuracy + After Image + Wraith Form+ + Phantasmal Killer + Shiv burst, and (4) Silent Dead Branch + exhaust engine (dump Powers Phase 1, zero Powers Phase 2).
+**CURRENT STATUS (163 runs, 5 WINS):** Three Ironclad victories (Runs 147, 148, 150). Two Silent victories (Run 158 vs Time Eater, Run 162 vs Awakened One). Thirteen Silent runs total (Runs 151-163). FOUR proven winning formulas now exist: (1) Ironclad Snecko Eye + Immolate+ + Limit Break, (2) Ironclad Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam, (3) Silent Accuracy + After Image + Wraith Form+ + Phantasmal Killer + Shiv burst, and (4) Silent Dead Branch + exhaust engine (dump Powers Phase 1, zero Powers Phase 2).
**SCORECARD (runs 101-158):**
- Act 1 boss wins: ~73%. All three Act 1 bosses beaten across runs.
- Act 2 boss kills: 6. Act 2 boss reaches: 12+.
- **Act 3 reached eight times (Runs 145, 147, 148, 149, 150, 151, 158, 162).** Run 145 died to Transient. Run 149 died to Giant Head elite. Run 151 (Silent) died to Awakened One Phase 2 at Floor 50. Runs 147, 148, 150 (Ironclad) and Runs 158, 162 (Silent) all WON.
- **Best floor: 51 -- VICTORY (Runs 147, 148, 150, 158, 162 all reached Floor 51).**
-- **5 wins in 162 runs (3.1% win rate).** Ascension 0. Three Ironclad wins. Two Silent wins (12 attempts: Run 162 VICTORY vs Awakened One -- Dead Branch exhaust engine, Run 158 VICTORY vs Time Eater -- Accuracy Shiv burst, Run 151 reached Act 3 boss, Runs 152/153/156/157 reached Act 2 boss, Run 154 died Act 1 boss Floor 16, Run 155 died Act 2 elite Floor 23, Run 159 quick death, Run 160 died Floor 28 to Hex deck clog, Run 161 died Floor 22 to Unknown room combat at 21% HP).
+- **5 wins in 163 runs (3.1% win rate).** Ascension 0. Three Ironclad wins. Two Silent wins (13 attempts: Run 162 VICTORY vs Awakened One -- Dead Branch exhaust engine, Run 158 VICTORY vs Time Eater -- Accuracy Shiv burst, Run 151 reached Act 3 boss, Runs 152/153/156/157 reached Act 2 boss, Run 154 died Act 1 boss Floor 16, Run 155 died Act 2 elite Floor 23, Run 159 quick death, Run 160 died Floor 28 to Hex deck clog, Run 161 died Floor 22 to Unknown room combat at 21% HP, Run 163 died Floor 19 to Malaise X-cost energy trap at 19 HP).
- **Run 162: Silent VICTORY Floor 51 vs Awakened One.** Dead Branch MVP. Phase 1 Power dump (all Powers played early, Curiosity Str resets on death). Phase 2 zero Powers -- Dead Branch + exhaust cards for infinite value. Lizard Tail consumed Phase 2 Turn 1 (41 dmg vs 20 block + 18 HP). Final HP 23/67.
- **Four distinct winning formulas confirmed.** See THE WINNING FORMULAS below.
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ High-level strategic principles for Ironclad runs.
3. **Build Act 2-ready decks by Floor 15.** ALL FOUR criteria: (a) front-loaded Strength (Inflame or Spot Weakness, NOT Demon Form alone), (b) AOE (Immolate is the highest-impact card), (c) healing beyond Burning Blood (Toy Ornithopter + potions is a valid backup), (d) block scaling beyond basic Defends.
4. **Pursue one of the two proven scaling engines.** (A) **Strength engine**: Snecko Eye as Act 2 boss relic, Immolate+ for AOE, Limit Break for Str doubling, Heavy Blade or other Str finishers. (B) **Block engine**: Barricade + Corruption + Feel No Pain + Entrench + Body Slam+. Immolate+ is the primary AOE in both. Do not force one engine -- pursue whichever the card/relic offerings support. If Barricade + Corruption + FNP are all available by mid-Act 2, commit to the block engine. If Snecko Eye is offered as Act 2 boss relic and Limit Break exists, commit to the Str engine. Either engine can defeat any Act 3 boss.
5. **Use the Act 1 tier list for card evaluation.** The starting deck is the same every run. Know the tier list, draft for the visible boss.
-6. **STOP MAKING ARITHMETIC ERRORS.** Before EVERY damage/block calculation, verify: (a) Who has Weak? (b) Am I using UPGRADED card values? (c) Strength resets between combats. (d) On kill turns, NEVER chain `end` with the attack -- verify the kill first.
+6. **STOP MAKING ARITHMETIC AND MECHANICS ERRORS.** Before EVERY damage/block calculation, verify: (a) Who has Weak? (b) Am I using UPGRADED card values? (c) Strength resets between combats. (d) On kill turns, NEVER chain `end` with the attack -- verify the kill first. (e) X-cost cards (Malaise, Whirlwind, Skewer) use ALL remaining energy -- play them LAST.
7. **Brimstone + Book of Stabbing = death.** Do NOT buy Brimstone if Book of Stabbing is possible. Book natively gains +3 Str/turn; Brimstone adds +2 Str/turn on top = +5 Str/turn. Two deaths confirmed.
8. **3 Cultists entry threshold is 60%.** Six deaths at 5-53% HP confirm. This is a hard-scaler -- kill speed > block.
9. **Enter every boss at as high HP as possible.** The first victory entered all three bosses at 80/80. The second victory entered Act 1 and Act 2 bosses at high HP but entered Donu and Deca at only 49% (42/85) and survived at 4 HP -- a razor-thin margin. Full HP entry provides the buffer needed when boss fights go long. Regal Pillow (+15 rest heal), smart pathing, and Omamori (free event rewards without curse downside) enable full HP entry. A powerful deck (Snecko Eye + Immolate+ + Limit Break+) can compensate for low HP entry, but the margin of error is minimal.
@@ -70,6 +70,12 @@ Before calculating damage or block, answer these FOUR questions:
- Confirmed fatal error: player played Iron Wave (13 damage) against 20 HP Hexaghost and submitted `end` in the same command line. Headbutt (17 damage) was in hand with 2E remaining. The combined 30 damage would have killed. The player wrote "13 > 20" in reasoning -- a basic comparison error that went unnoticed because the command was already committed.
- **RULE: On kill turns, NEVER include `end` in the same command as the attack. Play the attack, verify the kill, THEN end turn separately.**
+**5. X-COST CARDS USE ALL REMAINING ENERGY -- PLAY LAST.**
+- X-cost cards (Malaise, Whirlwind, Skewer) automatically consume ALL remaining energy. You cannot specify X manually.
+- To control X: spend energy on other cards FIRST, then play the X-cost card LAST with only the desired energy remaining.
+- Confirmed fatal error: player intended Malaise X=1 on Mugger but played it first with 4E remaining. Malaise consumed X=4, leaving 0E for Defend/Survivor. Took 21 unblocked damage at 19 HP = death.
+- **RULE: NEVER play an X-cost card before block cards unless you want X = all remaining energy.**
+
---
## HP Management: Full Block Is the Solution
@@ -451,7 +457,7 @@ Exception to the long fight problem: Fiend Fire + Unceasing Top creates a sustai
- After a brutal fight (Byrds, Centurion+Mystic), next room MUST be healing, not another combat.
- If the path forces an elite at low HP, skip it entirely -- take any alternative path.
- **Act 2 route must include a non-combat room in the first 2-3 floors.** The first Act 2 hallway fight can be 3 Byrds (50-73 HP drain), Spheric Guardian (30-41 HP drain), or other high-damage encounters. If the first 2-3 floors are all Monster rooms with no rest/shop/event escape valve, a single brutal fight leaves no recovery option. When choosing Act 2 entry points, prefer routes where a rest site, shop, or event appears by floor 2-3, even if it means giving up other rewards. A route of M-M-M before a rest site is a death trap.
-- **Unknown rooms are NOT safe in Act 2.** They can resolve as any hallway fight, including Byrds, Snake Plant, and Spheric Guardian + Sentry. FOUR deaths confirmed from Unknown rooms resolving as combat (at 21%, 26%, 43%, and 100% HP). Treat Unknown rooms as Monster rooms when routing in Act 2. Only take Unknown rooms in Act 2 if the deck has Strength scaling and the alternative path is worse. **At below 30% HP, Unknown rooms are lethal.** The player has repeatedly chosen Unknown rooms at critical HP reasoning "Unknown is safer than Monster" -- this is WRONG. Unknown rooms ARE Monster rooms ~50% of the time. At 21% HP with no potions, the correct choice is ANY non-combat room (rest site, shop) or, if none exists, the run was lost at map selection.
+- **Unknown rooms are NOT safe in Act 2.** They can resolve as any hallway fight, including Byrds, Snake Plant, and Spheric Guardian + Sentry. FIVE deaths confirmed from Unknown rooms resolving as combat (at 21%, 26%, 27%, 43%, and 100% HP). Treat Unknown rooms as Monster rooms when routing in Act 2. Only take Unknown rooms in Act 2 if the deck has Strength scaling and the alternative path is worse. **At below 30% HP, Unknown rooms are lethal.** The player has repeatedly chosen Unknown rooms at critical HP reasoning "Unknown is safer than Monster" -- this is WRONG. Unknown rooms ARE Monster rooms ~50% of the time. At 21% HP with no potions, the correct choice is ANY non-combat room (rest site, shop) or, if none exists, the run was lost at map selection.
### Elite Risk Assessment
@@ -577,7 +583,7 @@ HP is fully restored between acts (confirmed). After beating the Act 2 boss at a
2. **Bad map pathing** -- taking combat rooms after HP-draining fights. Solved by Full Act Pathing (plan the whole route before floor 1, re-route when HP drops).
3. **3 Cultists at low HP** -- 6 deaths at 5-53% HP entry. Hard-scaler exception: kill speed > block. 60% entry threshold non-negotiable.
4. **Book of Stabbing** -- 4 deaths. #1 elite killer. Double scaling (+1 hit/turn AND +3 Str/turn). Needs exhaust tools. Brimstone makes it worse. Silent decks without poison or Malaise should avoid.
-5. **Mechanics execution errors** -- arithmetic mistakes that waste 3-20 HP per error. Full Block Flowchart forces explicit math every turn, which should reduce these.
+5. **Mechanics execution errors** -- arithmetic mistakes and X-cost card misplays that waste 3-20 HP per error. X-cost cards (Malaise, Whirlwind, Skewer) consume ALL remaining energy automatically -- playing them first leaves 0E for block. Full Block Flowchart forces explicit math every turn, which should reduce these.
6. **Healing RNG denial** -- 7 of last 9 runs with no Reaper/Feed offered. Not directly addressable, but Full Block reduces the need for healing by preventing HP loss in the first place.
7. **Brimstone anti-synergy** -- 2 deaths from Brimstone + Book of Stabbing.
8. **Deck building window closes at Floor 15** -- missing criteria after F15 cannot be filled. Act 1 tier list + boss drafting should improve card selection.
Run 162: SECOND SILENT WIN vs Awakened One, Formula 4 (Dead Branch Exhaust)
54ad9741
2026-05-15
playbook/bosses/awakened-one.md
@@ -23,17 +23,23 @@ Each Power played adds +2 to every attack the Awakened One makes for the rest of
## PHASE 1
-The Awakened One starts with two Cultist minions (~48 HP each). Phase 1 has approximately 320 HP. After Phase 1 HP reaches 0, the Awakened One enters Phase 2 with full HP (300). All your Powers, buffs, and poison are REMOVED when Phase 2 begins.
+The Awakened One starts with two Cultist minions (~48 HP each). Phase 1 has approximately 300 HP (A0). After Phase 1 HP reaches 0, the Awakened One enters Phase 2 with full HP (300). All your Powers are STRIPPED at the phase transition, but their EFFECTS (Dex from Footwork, block-per-card from After Image, Caltrops thorns) appear to persist (confirmed in 1 victory, needs more data). Poison on the boss is REMOVED.
**Phase 1 priorities:**
- Kill the Cultists quickly. They scale with Ritual (+1 Str/turn) and deal increasing damage if left alive.
- Damage the Awakened One to end Phase 1, but conserve HP for Phase 2.
-- **Minimize Power plays.** Every Power played in Phase 1 makes Phase 1 harder by giving the AO more Strength. And all your Powers are stripped at the phase transition anyway, so their long-term value is zero.
-**When to play Powers in Phase 1:**
-- Playing 0 Powers is the ideal. Win Phase 1 with attacks, skills, and potions.
-- Playing 1 Power may be acceptable if it is game-defining (e.g., a Power that enables your entire win condition and you cannot survive Phase 1 without it).
-- Playing 2+ Powers in Phase 1 is almost always wrong. You are paying +4 or more Strength to the boss for buffs that get stripped at the phase transition.
+**When to play Powers in Phase 1 -- THE DUMP STRATEGY (confirmed in victory):**
+The Curiosity Strength gained in Phase 1 RESETS when the Awakened One dies and enters Phase 2. All player Powers are also stripped. This creates a unique strategic window: **play ALL your Powers in Phase 1, because the Strength cost resets on death.** The Powers help you kill Phase 1 faster (saving HP for Phase 2) and the Str given to the boss only applies to the remaining Phase 1 turns -- a short window.
+
+This was executed successfully in a victory: After Image, Footwork x2, Well-Laid Plans, Caltrops, and Noxious Fumes were ALL played in Phase 1. The AO gained Str from Curiosity, but the player applied Terror (Vuln 99) and burst it down quickly. The Str cost was irrelevant because Phase 1 ended before it accumulated to dangerous levels.
+
+**Phase 1 Power dump guidelines:**
+- Play Powers early in Phase 1 to maximize their benefit before the phase transition strips them.
+- Apply Terror/Vulnerable to convert the Power-boosted card plays into faster Phase 1 kills.
+- Use Piercing Wail and Weak to counteract the Str the AO gains from Curiosity during Phase 1.
+- The more Powers you play early, the faster Phase 1 ends, limiting the number of turns the AO gets to use its boosted Str.
+- **This ONLY works because Phase 1 Str resets.** Phase 2 is the opposite -- see below.
## PHASE 2
@@ -44,10 +50,11 @@ HP: 300. Gains Regenerate 10 (heals 10 HP per turn). Curiosity resets -- Strengt
- The boss also uses single large attacks and debuff/buff turns.
- Regenerate 10 heals the boss every turn, so the fight has a clock -- you must out-damage the regeneration or you lose to attrition.
-**Phase 2 Power discipline:**
-- **0 Powers is still ideal.** If your deck can kill a 300 HP boss with Regenerate 10 using only attacks and skills, play zero Powers.
-- **1 Power is the absolute maximum in most situations.** Only play it if it is your sole path to victory and you understand the Strength cost.
+**Phase 2 Power discipline (STRICT -- confirmed by victory):**
+- **0 Powers is the rule.** The victory run played ZERO Powers in Phase 2 and won. The player's hand frequently contained Powers (Noxious Fumes, Caltrops, Infinite Blades, etc.) that were deliberately not played. Instead, all damage came from Attacks and Skills + Dead Branch exhaust generation.
+- **1 Power is the absolute maximum in desperate situations.** Only play it if it is your sole path to victory and you understand the Strength cost.
- **Every Power beyond the first makes the fight dramatically harder.** 2 Powers = +4 Str = roughly +12 extra damage per multi-attack turn. 3 Powers = +6 Str = +18 per multi-attack. This adds up across every remaining turn.
+- **Dead Branch solves the "no Powers" problem.** Without Powers, the deck needs an alternative card generation engine. Dead Branch + exhaust cards (Terror, Piercing Wail, Adrenaline, Storm of Steel, Shivs, Endless Agony, Distraction) generates random cards every turn, providing both offense and defense without triggering Curiosity.
## HOW TO WIN WITHOUT POWERS
@@ -57,18 +64,27 @@ HP: 300. Gains Regenerate 10 (heals 10 HP per turn). Curiosity resets -- Strengt
- **Block without Powers:** Impervious, Defend, Backflip, Blur, Leg Sweep, Footwork (Footwork IS a Power -- weigh the Dexterity against the Str cost).
- **Weak application:** Neutralize, Leg Sweep, Sucker Punch. Reducing the boss's damage by 25% is often worth more than a Power that adds 2 Str.
-## WHAT KILLED US (Run 151)
+## WHAT KILLED US (Run 151 -- Silent, Phase 2 death)
Died Phase 2, Turn 14, 6 HP vs 16x3 = 48 multi-attack with only 28 block. The 16 per hit was 10 base + 6 Strength from Curiosity. Six Powers were played over the course of the fight, split across both phases. The Awakened One's Phase 2 Strength of 6 means 3 Powers were played in Phase 2 alone. Without those Phase 2 Powers, the multi-attack would have been 10x3 = 30, and 28 block would have left only 2 unblocked damage -- survivable at 6 HP.
-The deck contained 7 Power cards: After Image+, Infinite Blades, Well-Laid Plans, Tools of the Trade, Accuracy, Footwork, Noxious Fumes. The player declared the Powers "essential" and played them freely. This is exactly the wrong approach. The Powers played in Phase 1 were also harmful: they gave the AO Strength during Phase 1 (dealing more damage before the transition), and they were stripped at the phase transition anyway -- providing zero carry-over benefit.
+The deck contained 7 Power cards: After Image+, Infinite Blades, Well-Laid Plans, Tools of the Trade, Accuracy, Footwork, Noxious Fumes. The player declared the Powers "essential" and played them freely. This is exactly the wrong approach.
+
+## WHAT WON (Run 162 -- Silent, VICTORY, Dead Branch engine)
+
+**Phase 1 -- Power dump:** Played ALL Powers early (After Image, Footwork x2, Well-Laid Plans, Caltrops, Noxious Fumes). Applied Terror for Vuln 99. Used Flex Potions + Glass Knife for 42-damage burst attacks. Burst damage + poison killed Phase 1 in ~8 turns. The Curiosity Str gained was offset by Piercing Wail (-6 Str) and Weak from Neutralize/Crippling Cloud. Phase 1 Str resets on death, so all Powers were "free."
+
+**Phase 2 -- Zero Powers, Dead Branch engine:** Deliberately avoided all Power cards despite having them in hand. Damage came entirely from Attacks + Skills + Dead Branch random card generation. Key plays: Terror for Vuln 99 (exhaust = Dead Branch proc), Shivs from Cloak and Dagger exhausting for Dead Branch procs, Storm of Steel generating 3 Shivs that each exhausted for more Dead Branch procs, Adrenaline for free energy + Dead Branch proc, Distraction for Dead Branch proc + random Skill. Dex 7 (from Phase 1 Footwork x2 + Footwork+, which persisted -- Dex was NOT stripped) + After Image + Blur provided blocking. Lizard Tail saved the run on Phase 2 Turn 1 (41 damage vs 20 block + 18 HP). Final kill on ~Turn 7 of Phase 2 via Strike + Sucker Punch + Quick Slash burst.
+
+**Key tactical insight: Dex from Footwork persisted into Phase 2.** Powers are stripped at the phase transition, but their EFFECTS (Dex, After Image block-per-card, Caltrops thorns) appear to persist. This means Phase 1 Power plays are doubly valuable -- the boss loses its Str, but the player keeps the buffs. Needs confirmation across more runs.
## DECK BUILDING FOR THIS FIGHT
When the Act 3 boss is Awakened One (you can see this on the map):
-- **Deprioritize Power cards during drafting.** Every Power in the deck is a card you must discipline yourself not to play, wasting a draw.
-- **Prioritize Skill-based and Attack-based scaling.** Catalyst (Skill), Leg Sweep (Skill), Backflip (Skill) are all better picks than Power equivalents.
-- **If your deck is Power-heavy, this is the hardest Act 3 boss.** A deck with 5+ Powers that relies on those Powers to function will struggle badly.
+- **Without Dead Branch:** Deprioritize Power cards during drafting. Every Power in the deck is a card you must discipline yourself not to play in Phase 2, wasting a draw. Prioritize Skill-based and Attack-based scaling (Catalyst, Leg Sweep, Backflip).
+- **With Dead Branch:** Powers are WELCOME in the deck. Play them ALL in Phase 1 (dump strategy) for their full benefit. In Phase 2, ignore them in hand and rely on Dead Branch + exhaust for card generation. The more exhaust cards in the deck (Terror, Piercing Wail, Adrenaline, Endless Agony, Storm of Steel, Distraction), the stronger the Dead Branch engine. Dead Branch fundamentally changes the Awakened One from the hardest boss to a manageable one.
+- **Terror is the MVP card for this fight.** 0-1E for Vulnerable 99 (permanent 50% damage boost) + exhaust (Dead Branch trigger). Take Terror whenever offered if Awakened One is the Act 3 boss.
+- **If your deck is Power-heavy without Dead Branch, this is the hardest Act 3 boss.** A deck with 5+ Powers that relies on those Powers to function will struggle badly.
## PREPARATION CHECKLIST
@@ -78,6 +94,14 @@ When the Act 3 boss is Awakened One (you can see this on the map):
4. Sustain vs Regenerate 10 -- must deal >10 net damage per turn averaged across the fight. Catalyst+ on poison solves this. High Str + multi-hit solves this.
5. Enter at 70%+ HP. Phase 2 is a long fight (10-15 turns) with escalating damage.
+## DEAD BRANCH STRATEGY (confirmed victory)
+
+Dead Branch + exhaust-heavy Silent deck is the best approach against the Awakened One because it generates card advantage in Phase 2 WITHOUT playing Powers. The engine:
+1. **Phase 1:** Play ALL Powers (dump strategy). Apply Terror for Vuln 99, burst down Phase 1.
+2. **Phase 2:** Play ZERO Powers. Use exhaust cards (Terror, Piercing Wail, Adrenaline, Storm of Steel, Endless Agony, Distraction, Crippling Cloud) for Dead Branch procs. Each proc generates a random card. Shivs from Cloak and Dagger exhaust for more procs, creating chain reactions.
+3. **Block in Phase 2:** After Image (from Phase 1) provides +1 block per card played. Dex (from Phase 1 Footwork) boosts all Skill block. Blur carries block between turns. Combined, these provide 20-40 block per turn from Skills + After Image without playing any new Powers.
+4. **Damage in Phase 2:** Terror Vuln 99 makes all attacks deal 50% more. Shivs at (4 base + Dex) * 1.5 Vuln = 15+ each. Random Dead Branch cards contribute attacks. Storm of Steel -> Shiv chain for 45+ burst damage.
+
## POTION TIMING
- Save at least 1 potion for Phase 2. Phase 2 is the harder phase -- do not spend everything in Phase 1.

## Silent Skill Cards
- [Blur](blur.md)
- [Malaise](malaise.md)
+- [Terror](terror.md)
## Silent Power Cards
playbook/cards/bane.md
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+# Bane (Silent Attack, 1E, 7 damage / 10 upgraded)
+
+Deal 7 (10) damage. If the enemy has Poison, deal 7 (10) damage again (double damage).
+
+EFFECT: Bane deals its damage TWICE if the target is Poisoned. This is effectively 14 (20) damage for 1E against any Poisoned enemy -- one of the highest damage-per-energy ratios for a common card.
+
+WHY IT'S GOOD:
+- In any Silent deck with poison sources (Noxious Fumes, Deadly Poison, Envenom, Bouncing Flask, Poisoned Stab), Bane is almost always dealing double damage.
+- 14 damage for 1E (unupgraded) rivals uncommon attack cards. 20 damage for 1E (upgraded) is exceptional.
+- The double-hit counts as two separate attacks for relics like Kunai (Dex per 3 attacks) and Shuriken (Str per 3 attacks).
+
+TERROR SYNERGY: With Terror's Vulnerable 99 active, Bane on a Poisoned target deals (7 * 2) * 1.5 = 21 damage unupgraded, or (10 * 2) * 1.5 = 30 damage upgraded, for 1E. This is among the highest single-card damage outputs for 1E in the game.
+
+DEAD BRANCH CONTEXT: Bane can be generated randomly by Dead Branch. When it appears against a Poisoned, Vulnerable boss, it is one of the best possible Dead Branch outcomes.
+
+LIMITATION: Against enemies without Poison, Bane deals only 7 (10) damage for 1E -- below average. Do not take Bane unless the deck has reliable poison application.
playbook/cards/terror.md
@@ -0,0 +1,23 @@
+# Terror (Silent Skill, 1E / 0E upgraded, Exhaust)
+
+Apply 99 Vulnerable to target enemy. Exhausts.
+
+EFFECT: The target takes 50% more damage from ALL attacks for the rest of combat. 99 turns of Vulnerable means it never wears off -- this is effectively permanent Vulnerable for the entire fight.
+
+UPGRADE: Terror+ costs 0E instead of 1E. The upgrade is excellent -- a free permanent 50% damage boost to any enemy.
+
+WHY IT'S EXCELLENT:
+- 99 Vulnerable is the single strongest damage multiplier in the game for sustained fights. Every attack for the rest of combat deals 50% more damage.
+- Exhausts, so it does not clog the deck after use. The exhaust also triggers Dead Branch (random card generation) and Feel No Pain (block).
+- 1E cost (0E upgraded) for a permanent 50% damage increase is the best energy-to-value ratio of any damage-amplifying card.
+
+BOSS FIGHT VALUE:
+- Against bosses with 250-300+ HP, the 50% damage increase applied to every subsequent attack saves 3-5 turns of combat. This translates to 3-5 fewer turns of incoming damage.
+- Against the Awakened One: Terror in Phase 1 accelerates the kill before Curiosity Str accumulates. Terror in Phase 2 enables the kill without playing Powers. Confirmed MVP in a victory.
+- Against any boss: play Terror on the first turn you can spare 1E, then focus on damage. Earlier application = more total bonus damage.
+
+DEAD BRANCH SYNERGY: Terror exhausts on play, triggering Dead Branch for a random card. This makes Terror a "free" Dead Branch proc on top of its devastating debuff. In the Awakened One victory, Terror was played in both Phase 1 and Phase 2 (generated by Dead Branch in Phase 2), each time providing Vuln 99 + a random card.
+
+BANE SYNERGY: Bane deals double damage to Poisoned enemies. With Terror's Vuln 99 active, Bane on a poisoned target deals (base * 2) * 1.5 = 3x effective damage. Confirmed strong interaction.
+
+SINGLE-TARGET LIMITATION: Terror only applies to one enemy. In multi-enemy fights, the other enemies are unaffected. Prioritize the highest-HP target (usually the boss or elite).
playbook/relics/dead-branch.md
@@ -12,4 +12,18 @@ FEEL NO PAIN SYNERGY: With Feel No Pain active, every exhaust triggers both Dead
DECISION POINTS: Dead Branch is always a strong relic pick. When you have Dead Branch, prioritize cards that exhaust (Corruption, Second Wind, True Grit+, Burning Pact, Fiend Fire). Corruption becomes the highest-priority card to acquire when Dead Branch is in your relic pool.
+SILENT EXHAUST SYNERGY: Dead Branch transforms the Silent's many exhaust cards into an infinite value engine. Key interactions:
+- **Shivs exhaust after play** (they are Ethereal-like 0-cost attacks). Each Shiv played exhausts and triggers Dead Branch, generating a random card. With 4-6 Shivs per turn, Dead Branch generates 4-6 new cards per turn.
+- **Storm of Steel** discards hand and generates Shivs equal to cards discarded. Each Shiv then exhausts on play for Dead Branch. Storm of Steel -> 5 Shivs -> 5 Dead Branch procs = massive card generation chain.
+- **Terror** (0E, Vuln 99, exhaust) becomes a free Dead Branch proc on top of its devastating debuff.
+- **Endless Agony** (0E, exhaust, copies itself when drawn) provides repeatable Dead Branch triggers.
+- **Adrenaline** (0E, +2E, draw 2, exhaust) = free energy + draws + Dead Branch proc.
+- **Distraction** (1E, exhaust, generates random Skill) = Dead Branch proc + random Skill.
+- **Piercing Wail** (exhaust) = Dead Branch proc after the Str reduction.
+- **Crippling Cloud** (exhaust) = Dead Branch proc after poison + Weak AOE.
+
+The Silent Dead Branch engine won against the Awakened One (both phases). In Phase 2, the player avoided all Powers and relied entirely on Dead Branch + exhaust cards for card generation. Storm of Steel -> Shiv chains via Dead Branch created explosive turns dealing 45+ damage while generating block through After Image. This is a confirmed winning approach.
+
+DEAD BRANCH + AFTER IMAGE: Each Dead Branch-generated card played triggers After Image for +1 block. With 5-10 card plays per turn from Dead Branch chains, After Image generates 5-10 passive block per turn. This combination provides both offense (random cards) and defense (After Image block) simultaneously.
+
ANTI-SYNERGIES: None significant. Dead Branch is universally strong. The only risk is relying on random card generation for specific answers (you cannot control what cards you get).

High-level strategic principles for Ironclad runs.
-**CURRENT STATUS (161 runs, 4 WINS):** Three Ironclad victories (Runs 147, 148, 150). One Silent victory (Run 158 -- FIRST SILENT WIN). Eleven Silent runs total (Runs 151-161). Run 161 (Silent) died Floor 22 to Sentry + Spheric Guardian from Unknown room at 21% HP -- Sozu (no potions) removed all safety nets. THREE proven winning formulas now exist: (1) Ironclad Snecko Eye + Immolate+ + Limit Break, (2) Ironclad Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam, and (3) Silent Accuracy + After Image + Wraith Form+ + Phantasmal Killer + Shiv burst.
+**CURRENT STATUS (162 runs, 5 WINS):** Three Ironclad victories (Runs 147, 148, 150). Two Silent victories (Run 158 vs Time Eater, Run 162 vs Awakened One). Twelve Silent runs total (Runs 151-162). FOUR proven winning formulas now exist: (1) Ironclad Snecko Eye + Immolate+ + Limit Break, (2) Ironclad Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam, (3) Silent Accuracy + After Image + Wraith Form+ + Phantasmal Killer + Shiv burst, and (4) Silent Dead Branch + exhaust engine (dump Powers Phase 1, zero Powers Phase 2).

- Act 1 boss wins: ~73%. All three Act 1 bosses beaten across runs.
-- Act 2 boss kills: 5. Act 2 boss reaches: 11+.

-- **Best floor: 51 -- VICTORY (Runs 147, 148, 150, 158 all reached Floor 51).**
-- **4 wins in 161 runs (2.5% win rate).** Ascension 0. Three Ironclad wins. One Silent win (11 attempts: Run 158 VICTORY vs Time Eater, Run 151 reached Act 3 boss, Runs 152/156/157 reached Act 2 boss, Run 153 reached Floor 45, Run 154 died Act 1 boss Floor 16, Run 155 died Act 2 elite Floor 23, Run 159 quick death, Run 160 died Floor 28 to Hex deck clog, Run 161 died Floor 22 to Unknown room combat at 21% HP).
-- **Run 161: Silent died Floor 22 to Sentry + Spheric Guardian.** Sozu (no potions) removed emergency saves. Centurion+Mystic on F21 drained 24 HP (39->15) at 55% entry. Player chose Unknown room at 21% HP reasoning it was "safer than combat" -- playbook explicitly says Unknown = Monster in Act 2. Sentry + SG spawned. Survived 5 turns via excellent play (poison through Barricade, Piercing Wail+ negating attacks, precise block math) but died Turn 6 with 3 HP, 10 block vs 20 incoming.
-- **Three distinct winning formulas confirmed.** See THE WINNING FORMULAS below.

Phase 2 zero Powers -- Dead Branch + exhaust cards for infinite value. Lizard Tail consumed Phase 2 Turn 1 (41 dmg vs 20 block + 18 HP). Final HP 23/67.
+- **Four distinct winning formulas confirmed.** See THE WINNING FORMULAS below.
-**THE WINNING FORMULAS (confirmed by 4 victories across 3 distinct engines):**
+**THE WINNING FORMULAS (confirmed by 5 victories across 4 distinct engines):**
**Formula 1 (Ironclad): Snecko Eye + Strength Scaling (Runs 147-148).** Snecko Eye (Act 2 boss relic, draw 7, cost randomization) + Immolate+ (AOE) + Limit Break (Str doubler) + Heavy Blade / high-Str finishers. Key components: Snecko Eye as Act 2 boss relic, Immolate+ as primary AOE, Limit Break for Strength scaling. Works by rolling cheap costs on expensive cards and doubling Strength for massive finisher damage.
@@ -21,6 +21,8 @@ High-level strategic principles for Ironclad runs.
**Formula 3 (Silent): Accuracy Shiv Burst (Run 158 -- FIRST SILENT WIN).** Accuracy x2 (+8 Shiv damage) + After Image (innate via Bottled Tornado, 1 block/card played) + Wraith Form+ (3 Intangible for emergency survival) + Phantasmal Killer (double damage burst turn) + Malaise (X Weak + X Str Down for boss debuff) + Blade Dance (3 Shivs for 1E) + Finisher (damage per attack played this turn). Key components: Accuracy stacking makes every 0-cost Shiv deal 12+ damage; After Image as innate guarantees block from Turn 1 of every fight; Wraith Form+ provides 3 turns of Intangible when HP drops to crisis levels; Phantasmal Killer doubles the next attack for massive Shiv burst turns; Malaise neuters boss offense for the final stretch. Cultist Potion (Ritual 1) provides permanent Str scaling that compounds with Accuracy -- by Turn 10, Shivs dealt 23 each (4 base + 8 Accuracy + 11 Str). This formula defeats Time Eater because each Shiv is a single card play but deals massive damage, making the 12-card Time Warp counter manageable (12 cards can output 200+ damage per cycle). Lizard Tail provided death insurance for the revival that enabled the final burst.
+**Formula 4 (Silent): Dead Branch Exhaust Engine (Run 162 -- SECOND SILENT WIN, vs Awakened One).** Dead Branch (relic: random card on exhaust) + exhaust-heavy Silent deck (Terror, Piercing Wail, Adrenaline, Storm of Steel, Endless Agony, Distraction, Crippling Cloud, Shivs) + After Image (1 block/card played) + Footwork x2 (Dex 7 for massive Skill block) + Well-Laid Plans (card retention). Key components: Dead Branch transforms every exhaust into a new card, creating infinite card advantage; Terror provides Vuln 99 for permanent 50% damage boost; Storm of Steel discards hand for Shivs that exhaust into Dead Branch procs; After Image + high Dex provides blocking without Powers. **This formula specifically defeats the Awakened One** because it generates card advantage in Phase 2 without playing Powers (zero Curiosity triggers). Phase 1: dump ALL Powers (Curiosity Str resets on death). Phase 2: play ZERO Powers, rely entirely on Dead Branch + exhaust for card generation. Lizard Tail provides death insurance. Mercury Hourglass adds 3 passive damage/turn. Clockwork Souvenir provides Artifact 1 at combat start.
+
**What all formulas share:** (1) zero UI misplays, (2) zero arithmetic errors, (3) strong Act 1 performance, (4) proper map pathing, (5) a complete scaling engine (not individual strong cards). The key insight: pursue whichever engine the card/relic offerings support, not force one formula over the other.
**IMMEDIATE PRIORITIES (in order):**
@@ -33,7 +35,7 @@ High-level strategic principles for Ironclad runs.
7. **Brimstone + Book of Stabbing = death.** Do NOT buy Brimstone if Book of Stabbing is possible. Book natively gains +3 Str/turn; Brimstone adds +2 Str/turn on top = +5 Str/turn. Two deaths confirmed.
8. **3 Cultists entry threshold is 60%.** Six deaths at 5-53% HP confirm. This is a hard-scaler -- kill speed > block.
9. **Enter every boss at as high HP as possible.** The first victory entered all three bosses at 80/80. The second victory entered Act 1 and Act 2 bosses at high HP but entered Donu and Deca at only 49% (42/85) and survived at 4 HP -- a razor-thin margin. Full HP entry provides the buffer needed when boss fights go long. Regal Pillow (+15 rest heal), smart pathing, and Omamori (free event rewards without curse downside) enable full HP entry. A powerful deck (Snecko Eye + Immolate+ + Limit Break+) can compensate for low HP entry, but the margin of error is minimal.
-10. **KNOW YOUR ACT 3 BOSS MECHANIC AND BUILD AROUND IT.** The Act 3 boss is visible on the map from Act 1. Each Act 3 boss punishes a specific playstyle: (a) **Awakened One** punishes Power cards (+2 Str per Power played -- see awakened-one.md). If the boss is Awakened One, deprioritize Powers during drafting and plan to win with Attacks and Skills. (b) **Time Eater** punishes high card-count turns (12-card Time Warp -- see time-eater.md). Track card count exactly. However, Shivs with Accuracy stacking make Time Warp manageable -- each Shiv is 1 card play dealing 20+ damage, so 12 plays per cycle output massive total damage. The Silent Shiv archetype BEAT Time Eater in the first Silent victory. (c) **Donu and Deca** is a raw stats check (500 combined HP). The boss you see should influence every card pick, relic choice, and deck-building decision from Floor 1 onward.
+10. **KNOW YOUR ACT 3 BOSS MECHANIC AND BUILD AROUND IT.** The Act 3 boss is visible on the map from Act 1. Each Act 3 boss punishes a specific playstyle: (a) **Awakened One** punishes Power cards (+2 Str per Power played -- see awakened-one.md). If the boss is Awakened One, deprioritize Powers during drafting UNLESS you have Dead Branch -- Dead Branch + exhaust engine beats the Awakened One by dumping Powers in Phase 1 (Str resets) and playing zero Powers in Phase 2 (Dead Branch generates cards instead). Without Dead Branch, plan to win with Attacks and Skills. (b) **Time Eater** punishes high card-count turns (12-card Time Warp -- see time-eater.md). Track card count exactly. However, Shivs with Accuracy stacking make Time Warp manageable -- each Shiv is 1 card play dealing 20+ damage, so 12 plays per cycle output massive total damage. The Silent Shiv archetype BEAT Time Eater in the first Silent victory. (c) **Donu and Deca** is a raw stats check (500 combined HP). The boss you see should influence every card pick, relic choice, and deck-building decision from Floor 1 onward.

@@ -554,7 +556,7 @@ HP is fully restored between acts (confirmed). After beating the Act 2 boss at a
### What the Deck Needs for Act 3
-1. **A complete scaling engine**: Three proven winning approaches with four victories between them. **Ironclad (A)** Snecko Eye + Limit Break + Heavy Blade: burst-damage engine using cost randomization and Strength doubling. **Ironclad (B)** Barricade + Corruption + Feel No Pain + Entrench + Body Slam: block accumulation engine that converts persistent block to damage via Body Slam+. **Silent (C)** Accuracy x2 + After Image (innate) + Wraith Form+ + Phantasmal Killer + Malaise + Shiv burst: multiplier-stacking engine using Accuracy+Str on 0-cost Shivs with Phantasmal Killer for double damage burst turns. All approaches work against Act 3 bosses. The key is having a COMPLETE engine -- individual strong cards without the supporting pieces are insufficient.
+1. **A complete scaling engine**: Four proven winning approaches with five victories between them. **Ironclad (A)** Snecko Eye + Limit Break + Heavy Blade: burst-damage engine using cost randomization and Strength doubling. **Ironclad (B)** Barricade + Corruption + Feel No Pain + Entrench + Body Slam: block accumulation engine that converts persistent block to damage via Body Slam+. **Silent (C)** Accuracy x2 + After Image (innate) + Wraith Form+ + Phantasmal Killer + Malaise + Shiv burst: multiplier-stacking engine using Accuracy+Str on 0-cost Shivs with Phantasmal Killer for double damage burst turns. **Silent (D)** Dead Branch + exhaust engine: Dead Branch generates random cards from exhaust triggers; Terror provides Vuln 99; Storm of Steel + Shivs create Dead Branch chain reactions; After Image + Footwork provide blocking without Powers. Specifically effective against the Awakened One (zero Powers in Phase 2). All approaches work against Act 3 bosses. The key is having a COMPLETE engine -- individual strong cards without the supporting pieces are insufficient.
2. **Block density beyond Barricade**: Impervious, Shrug It Off, Defend+. Barricade needs block cards to feed into the snowball. Without block cards to play, Barricade only accumulates Metallicize's passive block (slow). Impervious + Barricade = 30-40 permanent block in one play.
3. **AOE**: Immolate+ is the single best card. Darklings require simultaneous kills; Exploders can be cleared with single Immolate+.
4. **Disarm/Weak sources**: Transient and scaling enemies require Strength reduction and Weak application. Save for Transient and boss fights -- do not waste on hallway fights that the Barricade engine can handle.
@@ -566,9 +568,9 @@ HP is fully restored between acts (confirmed). After beating the Act 2 boss at a
### What Wins and What Kills (Summary)
-**All four victories had:** (1) a complete scaling engine, (2) zero UI misplays, (3) zero arithmetic errors, (4) strong Act 1 performance. The fourth win (Run 158, Silent) introduced a completely new formula: Accuracy Shiv burst. Pandora's Box from Neow provided 2x Accuracy + Envenom + Infinite Blades. Bottled Tornado made After Image innate (guaranteed block from Turn 1). Ginger negated Doubt curse from Mind Bloom "I am Healthy" for a free full heal. Cultist Potion gave Ritual 1 for permanent Str scaling (reached Str 11 by Turn 10). Lizard Tail revived at 0 HP during the Time Eater fight. Key difference from Ironclad formulas: Silent wins through many small card plays (Shivs) with stacked multipliers (Accuracy + Str + Phantasmal Killer), not through single massive hits. Against Time Eater's 12-card counter, each Shiv deals 20+ damage, so 12 card plays per cycle still output massive total damage.
+**All five victories had:** (1) a complete scaling engine, (2) zero UI misplays, (3) zero arithmetic errors, (4) strong Act 1 performance. The fourth win (Run 158, Silent) introduced Accuracy Shiv burst vs Time Eater. The fifth win (Run 162, Silent) introduced Dead Branch exhaust engine vs Awakened One -- a formula specifically designed to defeat the Power-punishing boss by dumping Powers in Phase 1 (Str resets) and avoiding them entirely in Phase 2 (Dead Branch generates cards instead). Key difference from Ironclad formulas: Silent wins through many small card plays (Shivs, exhaust chains) with stacked multipliers (Accuracy + Str + Phantasmal Killer for Formula 3; Dead Branch + Terror Vuln 99 + After Image for Formula 4), not through single massive hits. Both Silent victories used Lizard Tail as death insurance in the Act 3 boss fight.
-**Every boss victory** had: (1) a card that addressed the boss's main threat, (2) passive block/sustain (After Image, Metallicize, Barricade), (3) entry HP above 50% or Pantograph or Lizard Tail, (4) at least 1 key upgrade or strong unupgraded cards, (5) no self-damage cards (Brutality/Berserk). **Every boss death** was missing at least one of these.
+**Every boss victory** had: (1) a card that addressed the boss's main threat, (2) passive block/sustain (After Image, Metallicize, Barricade), (3) entry HP above 50% or Pantograph or Lizard Tail (Run 162 entered at full HP with Lizard Tail as insurance), (4) at least 1 key upgrade or strong unupgraded cards, (5) no self-damage cards (Brutality/Berserk). **Every boss death** was missing at least one of these.
**Act 2 death causes (ranked by frequency, 35+ deaths):**
1. **HP attrition from failure to full block** -- the player takes unnecessary damage in hallway fights, then enters the next fight at critical HP and dies. Root cause: not executing the Full Block Algorithm. Solved by making zero-damage-taken the explicit default for every hallway turn.
Run 161 analysis: Sozu anti-pattern confirmed, Unknown room rule reinforced
69c51771
2026-05-14
playbook/enemies/centurion-mystic.md
@@ -28,3 +28,5 @@ POTION MANAGEMENT: Do NOT spend all potions on the Mystic kill. The Centurion so
AVOID: Attacking Centurion while Mystic lives. Entering below 50% HP. Wasting Fairy here when elites are ahead. Splitting damage between Centurion and Mystic even with temporary Strength buffs. Spending ALL potions on the Mystic kill phase.
OBSERVED FIGHT LENGTHS: This fight can extend to 10 turns with insufficient burst for Mystic. At 10 turns, even Metallicize and Weak on Centurion cannot prevent 30-65 HP drain. Stone Calendar (52 damage on turn 7) helps close out the fight before attrition becomes lethal.
+
+SILENT-SPECIFIC: The Silent can handle this fight with Poison + Dex stacking but expect 24+ HP drain even with correct play. Confirmed: Silent entered at 39/70 (55%), killed Mystic by Turn 5 via Backstab+Strike+Leg Sweep+Deadly Poison+Skewer burst. Malaise X=2 on Centurion (-2 Str, 2 Weak) neutered early attacks. Catalyst doubled Centurion's Poison (4->8) for sustained kill. Fight lasted 10 turns, drained 24 HP (39->15). Key resource decisions: (1) Piercing Wail+ used on Turn 10 to negate 8x3=24 multi-hit, saving 10+ HP but exhausting the card before the boss fight. (2) Leg Sweep blocked the first 8x3 multi-hit entirely (Weak + 27 block vs 18 Weakened). (3) Malaise should be played early on Centurion (Turn 3-4) to reduce cumulative damage across the long fight. The 55% HP entry was survivable but left only 21% HP for the remaining floors -- below the 30% threshold where Unknown rooms become lethal.
playbook/enemies/sentry.md
@@ -23,3 +23,5 @@ BRING: AOE (Whirlwind, Thunderclap, Cleave). Damage scaling (Inflame, Spot Weakn
AVOID: Slow play (Dazed accumulation cripples draws). Fighting with a starter-quality deck.
ACT 2 APPEARANCE: Sentries also appear in Act 2 hallway fights paired with other enemies (e.g., Sentry + Spheric Guardian). In these pairings, the Sentry still generates Dazed and attacks for ~9 damage per turn. Combined with Spheric Guardian's 10x2 attacks and Frail debuff, this is one of the most dangerous Act 2 hallway fights. Kill the Sentry first (no block to break through, lower HP) to reduce incoming damage count and stop Dazed generation. Gremlin Horn triggers on Sentry death, providing energy and draw to handle the Spheric Guardian.
+
+SILENT ACT 2 PAIRING: The Silent can handle the Sentry via Piercing Wail (negates beam damage entirely) and Terror (99 Vuln for fast kill). Kill order: Sentry first (strip Artifact with Neutralize, apply Terror for Vuln, burst with Backstab + attacks). Use Piercing Wail to zero the Sentry's beam turn while setting up Footwork Dex. After Sentry dies, poison the Spheric Guardian (Deadly Poison, Noxious Fumes bypass Barricade block entirely). Confirmed: Sentry killed Turn 4 via this approach. However, Dazed from Sentry Bolts combined with SG's Barricade block creates a lethal combination -- Dazed-clogged draws reduce block density while Barricade accumulates. At low HP (<30%), this fight is unwinnable regardless of deck quality. One death confirmed at 3 HP (10 block vs 20 incoming on Turn 6, insufficient block cards drawn due to Dazed dilution).
playbook/enemies/spheric-guardian.md
@@ -19,6 +19,10 @@ APPEARS WITH: Sentry (in Act 2 hallway fights). Spikers (in Act 3 hallway fights
SILENT-SPECIFIC: The Silent's Dex stacking (Footwork+) combined with Shiv damage (Accuracy + Blade Dance) can handle this fight but expect 26+ HP drain even with Dex 6. Kill the Sentry fast with Shivs (Accuracy Shivs deal 8 each, Blade Dance = 24 damage), then chip through Spheric Guardian's Barricade block over 4-5 turns. The critical danger turn is when both Footwork+ copies have not yet been played and no block cards are drawn -- a single turn with zero block cards against Spheric Guardian's 10x2 costs 20 HP. Tools of the Trade helps filter for block cards. Confirmed: 6-turn fight with 26 HP lost (59->33) despite Dex 6, Accuracy Shivs, and TotT active. The HP drain from this fight can set up lethal entry into subsequent forced encounters.
+SILENT POISON APPROACH: Poison bypasses Barricade entirely (Deadly Poison, Noxious Fumes). Confirmed effective: Deadly Poison (5 poison) applied through 65+ block, ticking damage each turn regardless of Barricade. Piercing Wail+ strips Artifact charges while also negating Sentry beam damage. However, the Silent's block density under Frail 5 is precarious -- 4x Defend with Frail produces only 20 block (5 each), barely matching SG's 10x2 = 20 incoming. A single missing block card at 3 HP is lethal. With Sozu (no potions), there is zero margin for error.
+
+DAZED + BARRICADE COMBO (Sentry + Spheric Guardian): When paired with a Sentry, this fight is especially brutal. Sentry's Dazed cards (Unplayable, Ethereal) clog draws, reducing the probability of drawing block cards on critical turns. Meanwhile, Spheric Guardian's Barricade accumulates block unimpeded during turns when the player draws Dazed-heavy hands and cannot deal damage. The combination creates a vicious cycle: Dazed reduces both damage output (block grows faster) and block density (more damage taken). Confirmed lethal at 3 HP when 20 incoming exceeded 10 available block due to Dazed-clogged hand.
+
ARTIFACT STRIPPING: Spheric Guardian starts with 3 Artifact charges. Uppercut+ is the best single-card Artifact stripper -- it applies Weak 2 and Vulnerable 2, consuming 2 Artifact charges in one play. Follow with any single debuff (Bash, Thunderclap) to strip the 3rd. Once Artifact is gone, Vulnerable massively increases kill speed through the Barricade block.
FRAIL 5 INTERACTION: Frail 5 reduces block from ALL sources by 25%. Defend gives 3 instead of 5. Shrug It Off gives 6 instead of 8. This lasts 5 turns and cannot be removed while Artifact charges remain. Combined with 10x2 attacks, the effective damage per attack turn is ~24+ (20 damage minus reduced block). This is the primary reason low-Strength decks lose -- block is insufficient AND damage is insufficient simultaneously.
@@ -29,4 +33,4 @@ WINNING FORMULA: Corruption + Feel No Pain engine (first survival, only 12 HP lo
CALIPERS INTERACTION: Calipers retains 15 block between turns, partially countering the Frail-reduced blocking problem. Impervious+ (40 block) retains 25 next turn with Calipers, providing substantial defense even under Frail. This combination significantly reduces HP loss from the 10x2 attack turns.
-WARNING: Single most dangerous Act 2 hallway fight. 4 deaths confirmed (at 26%, 43%, 47%, and 100% HP entry). 4 survivals: (1) Corruption+FNP engine (12 HP lost), (2) Immolate+Impervious++Calipers+Spot Weakness (20 HP lost), (3) Run 116: Inflame+Thunderclap+Bash++Flame Barrier++Armaments+ (38 HP lost over 14 turns, 60->22 HP). The third survival used Offering for early card draw, stripped all 3 Artifacts with 3 Thunderclap plays, then ground through Barricade block with Inflame (+4 Str total) and Vulnerable cycling. (4) Barricade+Impervious+Regen Potion+Bash++Rampage+Shockwave+Thunderclap (~32 HP lost over 8 turns, 80->48). Used Shockwave to strip 2 Artifacts, Intimidate to strip 3rd, then Barricade for permanent block and burst damage through Vulnerable. Regen Potion (5 HP/turn for 5 turns = 25 HP) was critical for sustaining through the HP drain. Even with correct play and front-loaded Strength, expect 30-40 HP drain. Key death causes: Demon Form too slow, Brutality self-damage in long fight, Rage+ expiring at end of turn, zero potions, Reaper useless (all damage absorbed by block = zero healing). Potions are critical -- entering with zero potions at any HP is near-certain death without Corruption+FNP engine or equivalent burst+defense combo.
+WARNING: Single most dangerous Act 2 hallway fight. 5 deaths confirmed (at 21%, 26%, 43%, 47%, and 100% HP entry). 4 survivals: (1) Corruption+FNP engine (12 HP lost), (2) Immolate+Impervious++Calipers+Spot Weakness (20 HP lost), (3) Inflame+Thunderclap+Bash++Flame Barrier++Armaments+ (38 HP lost over 14 turns, 60->22 HP). The third survival used Offering for early card draw, stripped all 3 Artifacts with 3 Thunderclap plays, then ground through Barricade block with Inflame (+4 Str total) and Vulnerable cycling. (4) Barricade+Impervious+Regen Potion+Bash++Rampage+Shockwave+Thunderclap (~32 HP lost over 8 turns, 80->48). Used Shockwave to strip 2 Artifacts, Intimidate to strip 3rd, then Barricade for permanent block and burst damage through Vulnerable. Regen Potion (5 HP/turn for 5 turns = 25 HP) was critical for sustaining through the HP drain. Even with correct play and front-loaded Strength, expect 30-40 HP drain. Key death causes: Demon Form too slow, Brutality self-damage in long fight, Rage+ expiring at end of turn, zero potions, Reaper useless (all damage absorbed by block = zero healing). Potions are critical -- entering with zero potions at any HP is near-certain death without Corruption+FNP engine or equivalent burst+defense combo.
playbook/relics/sozu.md
@@ -10,9 +10,16 @@ ANTI-SYNERGIES:
- **Woman in Blue event**: Cannot buy potions.
- **Healing potions as sustain plan**: With Sozu, the backup healing plan (buy Blood Potions at shops) is unavailable. Healing cards (Reaper, Feed) become even MORE critical.
+SILENT-SPECIFIC DANGER (3 deaths confirmed): Sozu is disproportionately dangerous for the Silent. The Silent lacks innate healing (no Burning Blood equivalent) and relies on potions as emergency saves in boss fights where survivability matters more than raw power. Three deaths with Sozu active:
+- Awakened One Phase 2: no Weak Potion or Block Potion to survive 48 multi-attack at 6 HP.
+- Bronze Automaton: lethal poison applied (2 turns from kill) but no potion to survive 2 more turns of 48+ incoming. Died at 3 HP.
+- Sentry + Spheric Guardian (Act 2 hallway): no potions to bridge the 3 HP crisis on the final turn. 10 unblocked damage from 20 incoming with only 10 block available.
+The pattern is consistent: Sozu removes the safety net that would have saved the run in a single critical turn. For the Silent, **strongly prefer other boss relics** (even Ectoplasm or Busted Crown) unless the deck has Wraith Form+ for Intangible safety and multiple block sources independent of potions.
+
HOW IT CHANGES DECISIONS:
- Skip all potion-related relics and events.
- Healing cards (Reaper, Feed) become absolutely essential since potions cannot supplement healing.
- Conservative play is more important -- no potions means no emergency saves.
- The extra energy partially compensates by enabling more block cards per turn, reducing damage taken.
- When offered as a boss relic swap from Neow, it is a strong pick IF you are confident in finding healing cards. The 4th energy transforms every fight.
+- **For the Silent: avoid Sozu unless the deck has Wraith Form+ AND After Image for potion-independent survivability.** The Silent's natural Skill-heavy block generation is vulnerable to Frail and bad draws, making potion safety nets critical.

Run 161 (Silent) died Floor 22 to Sentry + Spheric Guardian from Unknown room at 21% HP -- Sozu (no potions) removed all safety nets. THREE proven winning formulas now exist: (1) Ironclad Snecko Eye + Immolate+ + Limit Break, (2) Ironclad Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam, and (3) Silent Accuracy + After Image + Wraith Form+ + Phantasmal Killer + Shiv burst.

- Act 2 boss kills: 5. Act 2 boss reaches: 11+.

HP already 4/69 from the fight with zero recovery path.

+- **Run 161: Silent died Floor 22 to Sentry + Spheric Guardian.** Sozu (no potions) removed emergency saves. Centurion+Mystic on F21 drained 24 HP (39->15) at 55% entry. Player chose Unknown room at 21% HP reasoning it was "safer than combat" -- playbook explicitly says Unknown = Monster in Act 2. Sentry + SG spawned. Survived 5 turns via excellent play (poison through Barricade, Piercing Wail+ negating attacks, precise block math) but died Turn 6 with 3 HP, 10 block vs 20 incoming.

| Fight Type | Minimum HP to Enter | Notes |

| Hallway (easy) | 25% | Byrds/Chosen can drain 40-73 HP even with correct play and TC+ in deck |
-| Hallway (hard) | 60% | 3 Cultists (6 deaths, even 53% was insufficient), Centurion+Mystic (10x3 multi-hit can drain 61 HP, death at 71% entry confirmed), Snake Plant |

@@ -449,7 +449,7 @@ Exception to the long fight problem: Fiend Fire + Unceasing Top creates a sustai
- After a brutal fight (Byrds, Centurion+Mystic), next room MUST be healing, not another combat.
- If the path forces an elite at low HP, skip it entirely -- take any alternative path.
- **Act 2 route must include a non-combat room in the first 2-3 floors.** The first Act 2 hallway fight can be 3 Byrds (50-73 HP drain), Spheric Guardian (30-41 HP drain), or other high-damage encounters. If the first 2-3 floors are all Monster rooms with no rest/shop/event escape valve, a single brutal fight leaves no recovery option. When choosing Act 2 entry points, prefer routes where a rest site, shop, or event appears by floor 2-3, even if it means giving up other rewards. A route of M-M-M before a rest site is a death trap.
-- **Unknown rooms are NOT safe in Act 2.** They can resolve as any hallway fight, including Byrds, Snake Plant, and Spheric Guardian + Sentry. THREE deaths confirmed from Spheric Guardian spawning from Unknown rooms (at 26%, 43%, and 100% HP). Treat Unknown rooms as Monster rooms when routing in Act 2. Only take Unknown rooms in Act 2 if the deck has Strength scaling and the alternative path is worse. **At below 30% HP, Unknown rooms are lethal.**
+- **Unknown rooms are NOT safe in Act 2.** They can resolve as any hallway fight, including Byrds, Snake Plant, and Spheric Guardian + Sentry. FOUR deaths confirmed from Unknown rooms resolving as combat (at 21%, 26%, 43%, and 100% HP). Treat Unknown rooms as Monster rooms when routing in Act 2. Only take Unknown rooms in Act 2 if the deck has Strength scaling and the alternative path is worse. **At below 30% HP, Unknown rooms are lethal.** The player has repeatedly chosen Unknown rooms at critical HP reasoning "Unknown is safer than Monster" -- this is WRONG. Unknown rooms ARE Monster rooms ~50% of the time. At 21% HP with no potions, the correct choice is ANY non-combat room (rest site, shop) or, if none exists, the run was lost at map selection.
### Elite Risk Assessment
Runs 159-160 analysis: Hex vs Silent, Coffee Dripper entry, Chosen warning

playbook/enemies/chosen.md
@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@ DAMAGE:
KEY MECHANIC: Hex. Every non-Attack card played adds a Dazed to draw pile. Blocking is actively costly -- each Defend clogs the deck. Also applies Vulnerable to player, making Zap (18) and Poke (10) hit harder.
WHAT THIS MEANS: Minimize Skill usage. Front-load damage with Attacks. Bash+ is safe (Attack). Kill before Strength buffs make 7x2 lethal. With Vulnerable on you, even 7x2 base becomes 20 damage per turn. At low HP (<20), the 7x2 with Vulnerable is a death sentence.
-HP DRAIN: This fight routinely drains 30-40 HP even with a strong deck. If opening hand lacks block cards, the first 1-2 turns of unblocked 10-14 damage create immediate danger. At 50 HP entry, leaving at 17 HP is a realistic outcome -- which then makes the NEXT fight lethal (the death spiral pattern). Confirmed: 39 HP entry with Carnage+, Bash+, Headbutt, Dropkick, Demon Form, and Ghostly Armor resulted in 32 HP drain (39->7 HP) over 7 turns. Even with Demon Form scaling, the Chosen's Hex penalty on Skills and Vulnerable on the player made every turn painful. The fight is a reliable death spiral trigger in Act 2.
+HP DRAIN: This fight routinely drains 30-40 HP even with a strong deck. If opening hand lacks block cards, the first 1-2 turns of unblocked 10-14 damage create immediate danger. At 50 HP entry, leaving at 17 HP is a realistic outcome -- which then makes the NEXT fight lethal (the death spiral pattern). Confirmed: (1) 39 HP entry with Carnage+, Bash+, Headbutt, Dropkick, Demon Form, and Ghostly Armor resulted in 32 HP drain (39->7 HP) over 7 turns. Even with Demon Form scaling, the Chosen's Hex penalty on Skills and Vulnerable on the player made every turn painful. (2) Silent Cultist+Chosen at 30 HP entry with strong Shiv deck (ATC, Envenom, Footwork, Blade Dance+, 2x CnD+) resulted in 26 HP drain (30->4 HP) over 7 turns -- Hex added 5+ Dazed by Turn 4, collapsing block density. The fight is a reliable death spiral trigger in Act 2.
BENCHMARKS: Kill in 4-5 turns. Damage output drops from Weak -- plan accordingly. Must enter with 40+ HP minimum.
BRING: Attack-heavy deck. Iron Wave (Attack that blocks). Inflame (Strength overcomes Weak penalty). Bash+ (Attack, applies Vulnerable). Metallicize (passive block without triggering Hex -- Power, not a Skill).
@@ -21,4 +21,6 @@ AVOID: Playing Defend unless absolutely necessary (each one adds Dazed). Slow ki
FIEND FIRE WARNING: Fiend Fire can exhaust Dazed cards for damage, but it also exhausts ALL other cards in hand -- including defensive cards like Uppercut and Defend. If Fiend Fire does not kill the Chosen, the remaining turns have no block sources. At 14 HP, a single 28-damage attack with only 13 block available is lethal. Only use Fiend Fire when the burst will kill, or when the hand contains only expendable cards.
APPEARS WITH: Cultist (in multi-enemy fights). When paired, Cultist buffs turn 1 while Chosen applies Hex -- the combined threat is extreme. Kill Cultist first (lower HP, faster to remove, reduces incoming damage count). Once Cultist dies, Chosen alone is manageable with focused damage. Fire Potion or burst cards on Cultist turn 2 can eliminate it before combined scaling overwhelms.
-HEX DETAILS: Hex is applied turn 1. Each Skill played (Defend, Shrug It Off, etc.) adds one Dazed card to the draw pile. Dazed is Unplayable + Ethereal (exhausts at end of turn if in hand, but still takes up a draw slot). Over a 4-5 turn fight, playing 4-6 Skills adds 4-6 Dazed to the draw pile, significantly diluting future hands. This makes sustained blocking impossible -- the deck clogs with dead draws. Powers (Inflame, Rage) do NOT trigger Hex. Attacks are safe.
+HEX DETAILS: Hex is applied turn 2 (after Poke on turn 1). Each Skill played (Defend, Shrug It Off, etc.) adds one Dazed card to the draw pile. Dazed is Unplayable + Ethereal (exhausts at end of turn if in hand, but still takes up a draw slot). Over a 4-5 turn fight, playing 4-6 Skills adds 4-6 Dazed to the draw pile, significantly diluting future hands. This makes sustained blocking impossible -- the deck clogs with dead draws. Powers (Inflame, Rage, Footwork, After Image, A Thousand Cuts) do NOT trigger Hex. Attacks are safe.
+
+SILENT-SPECIFIC WARNING: Hex is disproportionately devastating for the Silent. Silent decks are naturally Skill-heavy (Backflip, Footwork, Survivor, Deflect, Blur, Piercing Wail, Acrobatics). In a Cultist+Chosen fight, Hex can add 5+ Dazed cards by Turn 4, diluting the deck to the point where critical turns draw 3-4 Dazed cards and 1-2 playable cards. The block density collapse is lethal when combined with Chosen's Vulnerable. Confirmed: deck with Footwork+, After Image, A Thousand Cuts, Envenom (all Powers, safe from Hex) still needed 3 Defends on Turn 3 (adding 3 Dazed), then Turn 4 drew too few block cards to survive. Silent mitigation: (1) Play Powers liberally -- they do NOT trigger Hex. (2) Front-load damage with Attacks and Shivs (Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger -- the block from CnD is a Skill but the Shivs are Attacks). (3) Use Piercing Wail Turn 1 BEFORE Hex is applied to negate damage for a free turn. (4) Kill the Chosen as fast as possible -- poison ticking while blocking minimally is the best approach.

playbook/relics/coffee-dripper.md
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+# Coffee Dripper (Boss Relic)
+
++1 Energy per turn. You can no longer Rest at rest sites (upgrade only).
+
+EVALUATION: Strong energy boost but removes the primary healing mechanism. Rest sites become upgrade-only, meaning the only healing sources are potions, events, healing cards (Reaper, Feed), and relics (Toy Ornithopter, Meal Ticket, Regen Potion, Blood Vial, Burning Blood). For the Silent, who lacks Burning Blood's passive healing, Coffee Dripper is significantly more dangerous than for Ironclad.
+
+WHEN TO TAKE:
+- Deck is nearly complete and rest sites would be used for upgrades anyway.
+- Multiple healing sources already exist (healing cards, potions, relics).
+- Energy-hungry deck that benefits greatly from the extra energy (Shiv archetypes, Barricade engine, multi-card turns).
+- Ironclad with Burning Blood providing baseline healing per fight.
+
+WHEN TO AVOID:
+- Early in Act 1 when HP management is uncertain.
+- No healing sources beyond rest sites.
+- Silent without healing cards or potions -- the Silent takes more chip damage and has no passive heal equivalent to Burning Blood.
+
+SILENT-SPECIFIC DANGER: The Silent relies heavily on rest sites for HP recovery because she has no innate post-combat healing (no Burning Blood equivalent). Coffee Dripper removes this safety net entirely. Every point of chip damage becomes permanent until a potion or event heals it. In Act 2, fights like Cultist+Chosen (30-40 HP drain from Hex deck clog) can drop HP to critical levels with zero recovery path. Confirmed: Coffee Dripper Silent entered Cultist+Chosen at 30 HP, dropped to 4 HP, then died two fights later because no healing existed.
+
+CRITICAL ANTI-SYNERGY -- COFFEE DRIPPER + ECTOPLASM: Taking both boss relics means no healing from rest sites AND no gold to buy potions or healing items at shops. This double downside eliminates two of the three primary recovery paths (rest, shop, card rewards). The only remaining healing is from card rewards and random events. This combination should be avoided unless the deck is already dominant and can reliably take zero damage in every fight.
playbook/relics/ectoplasm.md
@@ -20,5 +20,6 @@ ANTI-SYNERGIES:
- Golden Idol (+25% gold, negated).
- Bloody Idol (5 HP per gold gain, negated).
- Shops in general -- cannot buy potions, relics, or card removal.
+- **Coffee Dripper (CRITICAL)**: Coffee Dripper + Ectoplasm = no healing from rest AND no gold for potions/healing items at shops. This eliminates two of three primary recovery paths. Only healing cards and random events remain. Confirmed lethal: Silent with both relics dropped to 4 HP after Cultist+Chosen fight (Hex deck clog) and had zero recovery options, dying two fights later. Avoid taking Ectoplasm when Coffee Dripper is already equipped unless the deck can reliably take zero damage in every fight.
NOTE: Gold already in pocket is not lost -- it just cannot be spent (shops are still accessible for browsing but purchases require gold you cannot replenish). Spend all gold BEFORE taking Ectoplasm if possible. In practice, boss relics are offered after the boss fight, so spending gold at a prior shop is the last chance.

Coffee Dripper (no rest healing) + Ectoplasm (no gold) double boss relic downside eliminated all recovery paths. THREE proven winning formulas now exist: (1) Ironclad Snecko Eye + Immolate+ + Limit Break, (2) Ironclad Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam, and (3) Silent Accuracy + After Image + Wraith Form+ + Phantasmal Killer + Shiv burst.
**SCORECARD (runs 101-158):**
- Act 1 boss wins: ~73%. All three Act 1 bosses beaten across runs.
- Act 2 boss kills: 5. Act 2 boss reaches: 11+.
- **Act 3 reached seven times (Runs 145, 147, 148, 149, 150, 151, 158).** Run 145 died to Transient. Run 149 died to Giant Head elite. Run 151 (Silent) died to Awakened One Phase 2 at Floor 50. Runs 147, 148, 150 (Ironclad) and Run 158 (Silent) all WON.

-- **4 wins in 158 runs (2.5% win rate).** Ascension 0. Three Ironclad wins. One Silent win (8 attempts: Run 158 VICTORY vs Time Eater, Run 151 reached Act 3 boss, Runs 152/156/157 reached Act 2 boss, Run 153 reached Floor 45, Run 154 died Act 1 boss Floor 16, Run 155 died Act 2 elite Floor 23).
-- **Run 157: Silent died to Bronze Automaton at F33.** Fusion Hammer + Sozu from Neow. Poison strategy had lethal poison applied but deck could not survive 2 more turns. Automaton Str reached 9 by Turn 12+. Sozu blocked all potions -- no emergency healing.
-- **Run 158: FIRST SILENT VICTORY vs Time Eater at F51.** Pandora's Box from Neow gave 2x Accuracy + Envenom + Infinite Blades. Bottled Tornado made After Image innate. Ginger enabled free Mind Bloom heal (Doubt's Weak negated). Cultist Potion gave Ritual 1, Str scaled to 11 by Turn 10. Shivs dealt 23 each. Lizard Tail revived at 0 HP. Malaise X=5 neutered Time Eater's offense. Wraith Form+ played at correct timing (Turn 5 at 19 HP). Score 636, Final HP 47/70.
+- **4 wins in 160 runs (2.5% win rate).** Ascension 0. Three Ironclad wins. One Silent win (10 attempts: Run 158 VICTORY vs Time Eater, Run 151 reached Act 3 boss, Runs 152/156/157 reached Act 2 boss, Run 153 reached Floor 45, Run 154 died Act 1 boss Floor 16, Run 155 died Act 2 elite Floor 23, Run 159 quick death, Run 160 died Floor 28 to Hex deck clog).
+- **Run 160: Silent died Floor 28 to Cultist+Chosen.** Coffee Dripper + Ectoplasm double boss relic downside. Strong Shiv deck (ATC, Envenom+Snecko Skull, Strike Dummy, Footwork, Blade Dance+, 2x Cloak and Dagger+). Hex from Chosen added 5+ Dazed by Turn 4, collapsing block density. HP already 4/69 from the fight with zero recovery path.
- **Three distinct winning formulas confirmed.** See THE WINNING FORMULAS below.
**THE WINNING FORMULAS (confirmed by 4 victories across 3 distinct engines):**
Runs 157-158: FIRST SILENT VICTORY vs Time Eater, Shiv archetype documented
