-KEY MECHANIC: Double scaling -- hit count increases by 1 every turn AND Str increases by +3 every turn. This means damage per turn grows much faster than a single scaling mechanic. Example observed (with Str 3 start): T1: 24, T2: 18 (with Piercing Wail Str reduction), T3: 27, T4: 36 (Weakened), T5: 30 (Weakened), T6: 54 (unmitigated). Without debuffs, raw damage escalates to 50+ by turn 5-6. Combined with Wound draw clog, this creates a rapid death spiral.
+# Book of Stabbing (elite, Act 2, HP: 160-164)
+
+PATTERN: 85% Multi Stab, 15% Single Stab. Cannot use Multi Stab 3x in a row or Single Stab 2x in a row.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Multi Stab: 6xN damage (N = times used this combat + 2). Turn 1: 6x2=12, Turn 2: 6x3=18, Turn 3: 6x4=24, etc. (7xN at A3)
+- Single Stab: 21 damage (24 at A3)
+
+BUFFS: Painful Stabs -- whenever you receive attack damage, adds Wounds to your discard pile.
+
+HP: 160-164 at A0
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Multi Stab gains +1 hit every time it is used. Unlike what was previously documented, the scaling is from hit count increasing, not from Strength. Each Multi Stab adds more hits: 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5 -> 6. By turn 5-6, Multi Stab deals 6x6=36 or more. Combined with Wound draw clog from Painful Stabs, this creates a rapid death spiral.
THE WOUND CLOG PROBLEM: By turn 6-7, Wounds can comprise 60%+ of the deck. A 21-card deck accumulating 3+ Wounds per turn means that by mid-fight, you draw 3 Wounds and 2 playable cards per turn. This makes it impossible to find block AND offense in the same hand. Strong cards like Double Tap, Reaper, Inflame become buried under Wounds. The fight becomes unwinnable not because of raw damage scaling but because the deck stops functioning.
playbook/enemies/byrd.md
@@ -1,6 +1,16 @@
-# Byrd (hallway, Act 2, HP: ~25-30 each, appears in groups of 3)
-PATTERN: Attacks for 5-15 damage each (base 5-10, scales with Strength gained on buff turns). Has buff turns where they gain +1 Strength AND regain Flight 3. Attack types include Peck (1x5 multi-hit, 2x5, or 3x5) and Headbutt (single hit). By turn 4-5, individual Byrds can have Str 1-2, increasing Peck from 5 to 2x6=12 or 3x7=21. Late-fight all-attack turns can deal 28-38+ combined damage from 2-3 Byrds.
-KEY MECHANIC: Flight N. All damage is halved while Flight > 0. Each hit reduces Flight by 1. Multi-hit and AOE attacks strip Flight faster. Twin Strike+ strips 2 Flight in one play. Cleave strips 1 Flight from each Byrd.
+# Byrd (hallway, Act 2, HP: 25-31 each, appears in groups of 3)
+
+PATTERN (while airborne): 50% Peck (1x5 damage), 30% Caw (+1 Strength), 20% Swoop (12 damage). Cannot use Caw or Swoop twice in a row. Cannot use Peck three times in a row.
+WHEN GROUNDED (Flight removed): Stunned -> Headbutt (3 damage) -> Go Airborne (gains 3 Flying) -> returns to normal pattern.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Peck: 1x5 = 5 damage (each hit is 1, halved by Flight on other Byrds)
+- Swoop: 12 damage
+- Caw: 0 damage, gains 1 Strength
+- Headbutt: 3 damage (only when grounded)
+- Go Airborne: gains 3 Flying
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Flight 3. Takes 50% less attack damage while Flying. Each attack hit removes 1 Flight charge. Resets at start of next turn if not fully removed. Multi-hit and AOE attacks strip Flight faster. Indirect damage (poison, orbs) ignores Flight entirely.
WHAT THIS MEANS: Use Thunderclap to strip 1 Flight from ALL Byrds and apply Vulnerable simultaneously. Focus fire one Byrd at a time -- going from 3 to 2 removes 33% of incoming damage. Once Flight is at 0, burst with Vulnerable attacks to kill. Expect 7-12 turn fights and 20-73 HP lost.
BENCHMARKS: With Thunderclap + burst: 7-turn fight, ~20 HP lost. Without AOE: 8-10 turns, 36+ HP lost. Worst case observed: 8-turn fight with TC+ and Spot Weakness present, 73 HP lost (74 to 1) -- all 3 Byrds attacked on turn 2 for 38 combined damage with only 1 Defend in hand. This fight is the primary Act 2 HP drain and a leading cause of death spirals.
WARNING: If the map forces a Monster room immediately after a Byrd fight with no rest/shop/event option, the run may be lost. 3 Byrds can drain 50-73 HP even with correct play, and Burning Blood (+6) cannot compensate. When planning Act 2 routes, ensure the first 2-3 floors include at least one non-combat room (rest site, shop, event) as a safety valve after potential Byrd encounters.
playbook/enemies/centurion-mystic.md
@@ -1,6 +1,19 @@
-# Centurion + Mystic (hallway, Act 2, Centurion HP: ~76-80, Mystic HP: ~50)
-PATTERN: Mystic heals Centurion ~12 HP/turn and gains ~15 block. Centurion attacks for 12 damage on normal turns, but has a **10x3 = 30 damage multi-hit attack** on some turns. Both can have buff/defend turns (free damage windows).
-KEY MECHANIC: Mystic heals Centurion. Centurion is unkillable while Mystic lives.
+# Centurion + Mystic (hallway, Act 2, Centurion HP: 76-80, Mystic HP: 48-56)
+
+PATTERN:
+- Centurion (Mystic alive): 65% Slash (12 damage), 35% Protect (gives Mystic 15 Block). Cannot use any move 3x in a row.
+- Centurion (Mystic dead): 65% Slash (12 damage), 35% Fury (6x3 = 18 damage). Cannot use any move 3x in a row.
+- Mystic: Heals if any enemy missing 16+ HP (heals all enemies 16 HP, up to 2x in a row). Otherwise: 60% Attack (8 damage + 2 Frail), 40% Buff (all enemies gain 2 Strength). Cannot use Attack or Buff 3x in a row.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Centurion Slash: 12 damage (13 at A2)
+- Centurion Fury: 6x3 = 18 damage (only used after Mystic dies)
+- Centurion Protect: Gives Mystic 15 Block
+- Mystic Attack: 8 damage + 2 Frail
+- Mystic Buff: All enemies gain 2 Strength
+- Mystic Heal: All enemies heal 16 HP
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Mystic heals all enemies 16 HP when any enemy is hurt enough. Centurion is unkillable while Mystic lives. Once Mystic dies, Centurion replaces Protect with Fury (6x3).
WHAT THIS MEANS: KILL MYSTIC FIRST. Non-negotiable. If you attack Centurion while Mystic is alive, you're in an unwinnable attrition war. Focus all damage on Mystic despite Centurion being the bigger per-turn threat. Apply Weak to Centurion to reduce incoming while you kill Mystic. Expect 6-12 turns.
**CENTURION SOLO PHASE IS DANGEROUS.** Once Mystic is dead, Centurion still has the 10x3 multi-hit attack (30 damage). If you are at low HP after killing Mystic, this attack is lethal without strong block (Flame Barrier, Impervious, multiple Defends + Iron Wave). Do NOT spend all potions and exhaust cards on the Mystic kill -- save at least one defensive resource for the Centurion solo phase. Confirmed: death at 3 HP from 10x3 = 30 attack with no block cards or potions remaining.
playbook/enemies/chosen.md
@@ -1,6 +1,15 @@
-# Chosen (hallway, Act 2, HP: 95-98)
-PATTERN: Turn 1 applies Hex (adds Dazed to draw pile when you play Skills). Applies Vulnerable to player. Has a 7x2 multi-attack (14 raw, 20 with Vulnerable on player). Gains +3 Strength over the fight. Attacks escalate from ~10 to 14 (7x2) to 20+ as Strength stacks.
-KEY MECHANIC: Hex. Every Skill card played adds a Dazed to draw pile. Blocking is actively costly -- each Defend clogs the deck. Also applies Vulnerable to player, making its 7x2 hit deal 20 effective damage.
+# Chosen (hallway, Act 2, HP: 95-99)
+
+PATTERN: Always starts with Poke, then Hex. After that, repeats: (Debilitate 50% / Drain 50%) followed by (Poke 60% / Zap 40%).
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Poke: 5x2 = 10 damage (6x2 = 12 at A2)
+- Zap: 18 damage (21 at A2)
+- Debilitate: 10 damage + 2 Vulnerable (12 at A2)
+- Drain: 0 damage, applies 3 Weak + gains 3 Strength
+- Hex: Applies Hex debuff (whenever you play a non-Attack card, shuffle 1 Dazed into draw pile)
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Hex. Every non-Attack card played adds a Dazed to draw pile. Blocking is actively costly -- each Defend clogs the deck. Also applies Vulnerable to player, making Zap (18) and Poke (10) hit harder.
WHAT THIS MEANS: Minimize Skill usage. Front-load damage with Attacks. Bash+ is safe (Attack). Kill before Strength buffs make 7x2 lethal. With Vulnerable on you, even 7x2 base becomes 20 damage per turn. At low HP (<20), the 7x2 with Vulnerable is a death sentence.
HP DRAIN: This fight routinely drains 30-40 HP even with a strong deck. If opening hand lacks block cards, the first 1-2 turns of unblocked 10-14 damage create immediate danger. At 50 HP entry, leaving at 17 HP is a realistic outcome -- which then makes the NEXT fight lethal (the death spiral pattern). Confirmed: 39 HP entry with Carnage+, Bash+, Headbutt, Dropkick, Demon Form, and Ghostly Armor resulted in 32 HP drain (39->7 HP) over 7 turns. Even with Demon Form scaling, the Chosen's Hex penalty on Skills and Vulnerable on the player made every turn painful. The fight is a reliable death spiral trigger in Act 2.
playbook/enemies/cultist.md
@@ -1,6 +1,11 @@
-# Cultist (hallway, Act 1, HP: ~50-53)
-PATTERN: Turn 1: Ritual/Incantation (gains +3 Strength, does NOT attack). Subsequent turns: attacks with escalating damage (base 6 + 3 per turn of Ritual).
-KEY MECHANIC: Ritual +3 Strength per turn. Turn 1 is completely free.
+# Cultist (hallway, Act 1, HP: 48-54)
+
+PATTERN: Always starts with Incantation (gains 3 Ritual, does NOT attack). Subsequent turns: uses Dark Strike every turn (6 damage + accumulated Ritual Strength).
+KEY MECHANIC: Ritual 3 -- gains +3 Strength per turn permanently. Turn 1 is completely free.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Dark Strike: 6 damage (base, increases by 3 per turn from Ritual)
+- Turn 2: 9 damage. Turn 3: 12. Turn 4: 15. Turn 5: 18.
WHAT THIS MEANS: Go all-in turn 1 (Bash + Strikes for 17+ damage). Kill by turn 3-4 before attacks become dangerous. By turn 4, Cultist attacks for 15+.
BENCHMARKS: Kill in 3-4 turns. Turn 1 free damage: 17-21.
BRING: Bash for turn 1 Vulnerable setup, burst attacks.
playbook/enemies/darklings.md
@@ -1,12 +1,21 @@
-# Darklings (Act 3 Hallway)
+# Darklings (Act 3 Hallway, HP: 48-56 each)
-Three enemies. Each has moderate HP. Life Link mechanic: if any Darkling is killed while others survive, the killed Darkling revives at half HP at the start of the next turn.
+Three enemies. Life Link mechanic: if any Darkling is killed while others survive, the killed Darkling enters "Regrowing" state (cannot be targeted) for one turn, then revives at 50% HP.
## Mechanics
-- **Life Link**: Must kill all 3 Darklings simultaneously (same turn) or they revive at half HP. This is the defining mechanic -- partial kills are wasted damage.
+DAMAGE:
+- Nip: N damage (N = random 7-11, set at combat start; 9-13 at A2)
+- Chomp: 8x2 = 16 damage (9x2 = 18 at A2) -- only used by outer Darklings
+- Harden: Gains 12 Block (17 Block + 2 Str at A2)
+- Reincarnate: Revives at 50% HP
+
+PATTERN (outer Darklings): Turn 1: 50/50 Nip or Harden. Other turns: 30% Nip, 40% Chomp, 30% Harden. Cannot use Harden or Chomp consecutively; Nip cannot repeat 3x.
+PATTERN (middle Darkling): Same as outer but cannot use Chomp.
+
+- **Life Link**: Must kill all 3 Darklings in the same turn or they revive at half HP. Partial kills are wasted unless all die simultaneously.
- Each Darkling attacks independently. Combined incoming can reach 25-35 damage/turn.
-- Darklings can buff and debuff on non-attack turns.
+- Darklings can gain Block and Strength on Harden turns.
## Strategy
playbook/enemies/exploder.md
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+# Exploder (hallway, Act 3, HP: 30)
+
+PATTERN: Fixed sequence: Slam -> Slam -> Explode. Uses Slam twice, then detonates on Turn 3. The Explosive timer starts at 3 and decreases by 1 at end of each turn. When it reaches 0, Exploder uses Explode and dies.
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Explode deals 30 damage and kills the Exploder. This is a hard timer -- if you cannot kill the Exploder in 2 turns, you take 30 damage on turn 3. Explosion damage does NOT ignore block.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Slam: 9 damage (11 at A2)
+- Explode: 30 damage (kills self)
+
+BUFFS: Explosive 3 (countdown timer, decreases by 1 each turn)
+
+WHAT THIS MEANS: You have exactly 2 turns to kill the Exploder or eat 30 damage. At 30 HP, this is extremely dangerous. Kill priority is highest among the Shapes -- Spikers and Repulsors are annoying but not immediately lethal.
+
+STRATEGY:
+- KILL FIRST in any multi-enemy fight. 30 HP is very low -- most decks can kill in 1-2 turns
+- Front-load all damage on Exploders. Even at the cost of taking Slam damage (9), preventing the 30 explosion is correct
+- Poison can handle Exploders if applied heavily on Turn 1 (need 30+ total poison ticks in 2 turns)
+- AOE can efficiently damage Exploders alongside other enemies
+- Block the Explosion if you cannot kill in time -- it does NOT bypass block
+
+SILENT-SPECIFIC: Deadly Poison+ (12 poison) on Turn 1 kills the Exploder before detonation (12 + 11 = 23 by end of Turn 2... need supplemental damage). Noxious Fumes alone is too slow (2 + 3 = 5 poison by Turn 2). Best approach: direct attacks + Backstab for burst.
+
+DANGER LEVEL: High if not killed quickly. The 30-damage explosion can end runs at low HP. Low if you have burst damage or AOE.
+
+APPEARS WITH: Other Shapes (Spikers, Repulsors) in the 3 Shapes encounter.
playbook/enemies/fungi-beast.md
@@ -1,6 +1,15 @@
# Fungi Beast (hallway, Act 1-2, HP: 22-28, appears in pairs or with Shelled Parasite)
-PATTERN: Alternates between attacking (~6 base damage) and buffing (+3 Strength per buff turn). On death, applies Spore Cloud: 2 Vulnerable to the player.
-KEY MECHANIC: Strength scaling from buff turns + Spore Cloud on death. Each buff turn gives +3 Str, so attacks escalate: Turn 1 buff (free), Turn 2 attack for 6+3=9, Turn 3 buff (free), Turn 4 attack for 6+6=12, Turn 5 buff (free), Turn 6 attack for 6+9=15. Two Fungi Beasts that both attack on the same turn deal combined 18 (early) to 27+ (late). The fight becomes lethal if it drags past Turn 5-6 due to compounding Strength.
+
+PATTERN: Each turn, 60% chance Bite, 40% chance Grow. Cannot use Bite 3 times in a row. Cannot use Grow twice in a row.
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Strength scaling from Grow (+3 Str per use) + Spore Cloud 2 on death (applies 2 Vulnerable to player).
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Bite: 6 damage (base, scales with accumulated Strength)
+- Grow: Gains 3 Strength
+- Spore Cloud 2: On death, applies 2 Vulnerable to player
+
+Strength scaling example: After 1 Grow = 9 Bite, after 2 Grows = 12 Bite, after 3 Grows = 15 Bite. Two Fungi Beasts that both attack on the same turn deal combined 18 (early) to 27+ (late). The fight becomes lethal if it drags past Turn 5-6 due to compounding Strength.
WHAT THIS MEANS: Kill fast. Unlike static enemies, Fungi Beasts scale with time. A 6-turn fight against 2 Fungi Beasts faces 27 combined damage on the final turn. Kill one when the other is NOT attacking to avoid Spore Cloud Vulnerability + attack in the same turn.
BENCHMARKS: Must kill within 5-6 turns or Str scaling makes incoming unblockable. HP is higher than documented in some pairings (observed 22-28 HP range in Act 1).
BRING: Enough burst to kill one in 3-4 turns. Block for the escalating later turns. AOE is excellent (both are low-HP, AOE shortens the fight before scaling gets dangerous).
playbook/enemies/giant-head.md
@@ -1,16 +1,21 @@
-# Giant Head (Elite, Act 3)
+# Giant Head (Elite, Act 3, HP: 500)
-HP: 625. Single target. Very long fight.
+Single target. Very long fight.
-KEY MECHANIC: **Slow** -- each card played in a turn increases a counter. Attack damage is reduced based on how many cards you've played that turn. This punishes wide turns with many card plays. Play fewer, bigger cards.
+POWER: **Slow** -- whenever you play a card, the Giant Head receives 10% more damage from Attacks this turn. This actually REWARDS playing many cards per turn -- more plays = more damage dealt to it.
-ATTACK PATTERN: Giant Head starts with low attacks (~13) but escalates by +5 every attack turn. By turn 8-10, attacks reach 50-60+, making the fight a DPS race.
+PATTERN: For the first 4 turns, uses Count (13 damage) or Glare (1 Weak) with 50/50 chance (cannot repeat either 3x in a row). After 4 turns, exclusively uses "It Is Time" for the rest of combat.
-DEBUFF TURN: Giant Head alternates attacks with a debuff turn (applies Weak, possibly status cards). This is a free damage turn with no incoming attack.
+DAMAGE:
+- Count: 13 damage
+- Glare: 0 damage, applies 1 Weak
+- It Is Time: 30 damage base, increases by 5 per use (30 -> 35 -> 40 -> 45 -> 50 -> 55 -> 60 max)
+
+KEY MECHANIC: It Is Time escalates by +5 per use. After 4 turns of Count/Glare, every subsequent turn deals 30+ and growing. The fight is a DPS race -- kill before It Is Time reaches 50+.
STRATEGY:
-- **Demon Form is critical.** Must be played Turn 1-2. At 625 HP, you need exponential Strength scaling to kill Giant Head before its attacks become unsurvivable. Delaying Demon Form by even 2-3 turns can be fatal.
-- **Minimize card plays per turn** due to Slow. Play 1-2 big attacks rather than 3-4 small ones.
+- **Demon Form is critical.** Must be played Turn 1-2. At 500 HP, you need exponential Strength scaling to kill Giant Head before its attacks become unsurvivable. Delaying Demon Form by even 2-3 turns can be fatal.
+- **Slow HELPS you** -- each card played makes Giant Head take 10% more damage from attacks this turn. Play many cards per turn to maximize damage. Shivs and multi-card turns are rewarded.
- **Immolate+ with high Strength** is the best damage card -- single play, massive damage.
- **Entrench+ is essential** for surviving high-damage turns. Double your block to keep pace with escalating attacks.
- **Impervious** gives 30 block but exhausts. Save for the turn attacks spike past your normal block capacity.
@@ -18,10 +23,10 @@ STRATEGY:
HP THRESHOLD: Enter this fight above 60% HP. The fight lasts 10+ turns with increasing damage. Even with good block, expect to take chip damage from turns where attacks exceed your block capacity.
-DEATH PATTERN (Run 149): Entered at 53/80 (66%). Accidentally exhausted Reaper+ Turn 1 due to index shifting. Demon Form delayed to Turn 4. By Turn 10, Giant Head was dealing 55+ damage/turn but had only lost ~250 of 625 HP. Fairy in a Bottle triggered Turn 10 (revive to 24 HP). Died Turn 11 to 60 damage with only 27 block. Lost the DPS race because Reaper and Demon Form were both unavailable early.
+DEATH PATTERN (Run 149): Entered at 53/80 (66%). Accidentally exhausted Reaper+ Turn 1 due to index shifting. Demon Form delayed to Turn 4. By Turn 10, Giant Head was dealing 55+ damage/turn but had only lost ~250 of 500 HP. Fairy in a Bottle triggered Turn 10 (revive to 24 HP). Died Turn 11 to 60 damage with only 27 block. Lost the DPS race because Reaper and Demon Form were both unavailable early.
LESSON: **Never use turn() sequences with cards that shift indices.** Play one card at a time with state() checks between plays. A single index-shift error can exhaust a critical card and lose the fight.
-BARRICADE ENGINE APPROACH (CONFIRMED WIN): The Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam engine can defeat Giant Head without Demon Form or Strength scaling. Body Slam+ with 66-83 accumulated block deals massive single-card damage (bypasses Slow penalty since it is one card play). Apotheosis on the setup turn upgrades all cards. Entrench+ doubles block from 63 to 126, then Body Slam+ delivers the killing blow. With Preserved Insect reducing HP by 25%, the effective HP is ~469. The engine completed the kill in 8 turns with zero HP lost after the block wall was established. The key advantage: Body Slam+ is a SINGLE card play per turn, so Slow penalty is minimal.
+BARRICADE ENGINE APPROACH (CONFIRMED WIN): The Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam engine can defeat Giant Head without Demon Form or Strength scaling. Body Slam+ with 66-83 accumulated block deals massive single-card damage (bypasses Slow penalty since it is one card play). Apotheosis on the setup turn upgrades all cards. Entrench+ doubles block from 63 to 126, then Body Slam+ delivers the killing blow. With Preserved Insect reducing HP by 25%, the effective HP is ~375. The engine completed the kill in 8 turns with zero HP lost after the block wall was established. The key advantage: Body Slam+ is a SINGLE card play per turn, so Slow penalty is minimal.
NOTE: Demon Form is NOT required to kill Giant Head. Either Demon Form (Strength scaling) or Barricade + Body Slam (block scaling) works. The fight is unwinnable only when NEITHER scaling engine is available.
playbook/enemies/gremlin-gang.md
@@ -1,18 +1,18 @@
# Gremlin Gang (Act 1 Hallway)
-A mixed group of 4 gremlins drawn from different types. Not to be confused with Gremlin Leader (an Act 2 elite with a distinct Rally mechanic).
+A mixed group of 4 gremlins randomly selected from: 2 Fat, 2 Sneaky, 2 Mad, 1 Shield, 1 Wizard. Not to be confused with Gremlin Leader (an Act 2 elite with a distinct Rally mechanic).
GREMLIN TYPES (kill priority order):
-1. **Sneaky Gremlin** -- KILL FIRST. Glass cannon. Attacks for 9-10 damage per turn with low HP (~10-13). Left alive for multiple turns, cumulative damage is devastating (25+ damage observed from a single Sneaky Gremlin that survived 3 turns). Always attacks -- never buffs or defends. The highest single-target damage output of any gremlin type.
+1. **Sneaky Gremlin** (HP: 10-14) -- KILL FIRST. Glass cannon. Puncture: 9 damage every turn. Always attacks -- never buffs or defends. The highest single-target damage output of any gremlin type.
-2. **Gremlin Wizard** -- KILL SECOND. Charges a large attack over multiple turns (UNKNOWN intent while charging, then a big hit of 25-30). If the charge completes, the single hit can be lethal at low HP. Kill before the charge finishes. Lower priority than Sneaky Gremlin because the damage is delayed.
+2. **Gremlin Wizard** (HP: 21-25) -- KILL SECOND. Charges for 2 turns (Charging: no effect), then fires Ultimate Blast (25 damage). After first cycle, charges for 3 turns before each subsequent Ultimate Blast. Kill before the charge completes.
-3. **Fat Gremlin** -- MEDIUM PRIORITY. Attacks for 4-5 damage and applies Weak or Frail. Lower damage per turn than Sneaky Gremlin but the debuffs reduce your effectiveness.
+3. **Fat Gremlin** (HP: 13-17) -- MEDIUM PRIORITY. Smash: 4 damage + 1 Weak every turn. Lower damage but debuff reduces your effectiveness.
-4. **Shield Gremlin** -- LOWEST PRIORITY. Gives block to other gremlins. Annoying but not directly threatening. Kill last.
+4. **Mad Gremlin** (HP: 20-24) -- MEDIUM PRIORITY. Scratch: 4 damage every turn. Has Angry 1 (gains 1 Strength when taking damage). Do not spread incidental damage to it -- either commit to killing it or ignore it.
-5. **Mad Gremlin** -- MEDIUM PRIORITY. Attacks with moderate damage. Has Angry (gains Str when hit). Do not spread incidental damage to it -- either commit to killing it or ignore it.
+5. **Shield Gremlin** (HP: 12-15) -- LOWEST PRIORITY. Protect: gives 7 Block to a random ally every turn. Uses Shield Bash (6 damage) when alone. Annoying but not directly threatening. Kill last.
STRATEGY:
- **Turn 1**: Neutralize or Weak the highest-damage attacking gremlin (usually Sneaky Gremlin). Kill it with remaining energy if possible. Block the rest.
playbook/enemies/gremlin-leader.md
@@ -1,5 +1,20 @@
-# Gremlin Leader (elite, Act 2, HP: ~131)
-PATTERN: Starts with 2 gremlins of varying types (Fat Gremlin, Shield Gremlin, Sneaky Gremlin, Mad Gremlin, etc.). Uses DEFEND_BUFF (Rally): gives ALL enemies +3 Strength permanently and stacking. Gremlins attack for base damage + accumulated Str. Leader itself attacks for 6x3 base (18 damage) when not rallying. Leader re-summons gremlins when they die -- killing gremlins is temporary relief, not permanent removal.
+# Gremlin Leader (elite, Act 2, HP: 140-148)
+
+PATTERN: Move selection depends on gremlin count:
+- 0 gremlins: Rally! 75% / Stab 25%
+- 1 gremlin (last was Encourage): Rally! 50% / Stab 50%
+- 1 gremlin (last was Stab): Rally! 62.5% / Encourage 37.5%
+- 2+ gremlins: Encourage 66% / Stab 34%
+Cannot use any intent twice in a row.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Stab: 6x3 = 18 damage
+- Encourage: All enemies gain 3 Strength + other gremlins gain 6 Block (Str scales to 4-5, Block to 10-18 at higher Ascensions)
+- Rally!: Summons 2 random Gremlins (max 3 small gremlins at a time)
+
+HP: 140-148 at A0, 145-155 at A8
+
+Starts with 2 gremlins already present. Leader re-summons gremlins when they die -- killing gremlins is temporary relief, not permanent removal.
KEY MECHANIC: Rally gives +3 Str to ALL enemies (Leader + all gremlins). Permanent and stacking. After 2 rallies: every enemy has +6 Str. The fight's damage scales exponentially with turn count. With +3 Str after one rally, gremlins deal 9+3=12 each. With two gremlins attacking, that is 24 damage from gremlins alone, plus the Leader's own attacks.
RALLY SCALING MATH: Rally fires roughly every other turn. By turn 3, all enemies have +3 Str. By turn 5, all enemies have +6 Str. By turn 7, all enemies have +9 Str. With 2 gremlins alive at +9 Str, gremlins alone deal (9+9)*2 = 36, plus the Leader's own attacks. Total incoming by turn 7-8 exceeds 50 per turn. Every wasted turn is effectively +3 Str to the entire board -- a single lost turn (e.g., ending turn early after Battle Trance draw without playing drawn cards) can be the difference between winning and losing.
WHAT THIS MEANS: TURN ECONOMY IS EVERYTHING. Every turn must deal maximum damage or apply critical debuffs. Wasting even one turn (misplaying end-of-turn, failing to use drawn cards) allows Rally to stack once more, which compounds across all gremlins. KILL GREMLINS FAST to reduce per-turn damage, but prioritize Leader damage when possible because gremlins get re-summoned. AOE is critical for clearing gremlins efficiently. Apply Shockwave early for mass Weak + Vulnerable. The fight becomes mathematically unwinnable once Strength stacks too high (by turn 3-4 with multiple gremlins alive, incoming damage is 40+).
playbook/enemies/gremlin-nob.md
@@ -1,6 +1,15 @@
-# Gremlin Nob (elite, Act 1, HP: 85)
-PATTERN: Turn 1: buffs (no attack). Subsequent turns: attacks for 14+ damage, scaling with Enrage stacks. When player plays a Skill card, gains +2 Strength (permanent, stacking).
-KEY MECHANIC: Enrage. Every Skill card played gives Nob +2 Strength permanently. Powers do NOT trigger Enrage.
+# Gremlin Nob (elite, Act 1, HP: 82-86)
+
+PATTERN: Always starts with Bellow (gains Enrage 2). After that: 33% Skull Bash, 67% Rush. Cannot use Rush three times in a row.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Bellow: Gains Enrage 2 (whenever you play a Skill, Nob gains 2 Strength)
+- Skull Bash: 6 damage + 2 Vulnerable (8 at A2)
+- Rush (Bull Rush): 14 damage (16 at A2)
+
+HP: 82-86 at A0, 85-90 at A8
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Enrage 2. Every Skill card played gives Nob +2 Strength permanently. Powers do NOT trigger Enrage.
WHAT THIS MEANS: Play ZERO Skills. No Defend, no True Grit, no Shrug It Off, no Shockwave, no Impervious. Only Attacks and Powers. Use potions for block instead of Skill cards. Turn 1 is free -- go all-in on damage.
BENCHMARKS: 3-4 turn kill with zero Skills. Strength Potion on free turn 1 maximizes value. Playing 3 Skills gives Nob +6 Str, turning a manageable fight into near-death.
BRING: Attack-heavy hand. Bash (Attack, safe). Potions for block/survival instead of Skills.
playbook/enemies/jaw-worm-horde.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Jaw Worm Horde (hallway, Act 3, HP: 40-44 each, 3 enemies)
+
+Three Jaw Worms that start combat with the effects of Bellow already applied (+3 Strength and 6 Block each). This is an Act 3 Hard Pool encounter.
+
+PATTERN: Unlike Act 1 Jaw Worm (which always opens with Chomp), Act 3 Jaw Worms start with Bellow effects pre-applied and their first action is randomized: 45% Bellow, 30% Thrash, 25% Chomp.
+
+MOVES (same as regular Jaw Worm):
+- Chomp: 11 damage (12 at A2)
+- Bellow: Gains 3 Strength + 6 Block (4 Str + 6 Block at A2)
+- Thrash: 7 damage + 5 Block
+
+Starting state: Each Jaw Worm begins with +3 Strength (from pre-applied Bellow). So Turn 1 Chomp deals 14, Thrash deals 10.
+
+AI after first action:
+- After Chomp: 59% Bellow, 41% Thrash
+- After Bellow: 56% Thrash, 44% Chomp
+- After Thrash: 45% Bellow, 30% Thrash, 25% Chomp (cannot Thrash 3x in a row: 64% Bellow, 36% Chomp)
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Triple independent Strength scaling. Each Jaw Worm gains Strength independently. Starting at +3 each, a single Bellow round pushes them to +6 each. Combined damage escalates rapidly: Turn 1 with all attacking = 30-42 combined, Turn 3 = 40-60+ depending on Bellow count.
+
+WHAT THIS MEANS: This is a DPS race against triple scaling. AOE is critical for managing three 40-44 HP targets. Focus fire kills one quickly to reduce incoming by 33%.
+
+STRATEGY:
+- AOE is mandatory. Die Die Die, Blade Dance + Accuracy, Dagger Spray, Corpse Explosion
+- Focus fire one Jaw Worm while AOE chips the others
+- Apply Weak or Piercing Wail to reduce combined incoming on big attack turns
+- Kill order: whichever has lowest HP first, then next lowest
+- The fight becomes unmanageable after 4-5 turns if all 3 are still alive and scaling
+
+SILENT-SPECIFIC: Noxious Fumes applies poison to all 3 simultaneously. Piercing Wail reduces all 3 enemies' Strength. Die Die Die+ for burst AOE. Blade Dance provides multi-target damage. Wraith Form for emergency Intangible if scaling gets out of control.
+
+DANGER LEVEL: High. Triple Strength scaling makes this one of the most dangerous Act 3 hallway fights. Combined Turn 1 damage can be 30+ with no free setup turn.
+
+APPEARS WITH: Solo encounter (3 Jaw Worms only).
playbook/enemies/jaw-worm.md
@@ -1,7 +1,17 @@
-# Jaw Worm (hallway, Act 1, HP: ~40-44)
-PATTERN: Turn 1 uncertain (may attack or buff). Has buff/defend turns where it curls up (gains ~5 block) and/or buffs Strength. Attacks for 7-11 damage, increasing over time from Strength buffs.
-KEY MECHANIC: Strength scaling. Damage increases each turn the fight goes on.
-WHAT THIS MEANS: Kill fast. Apply Vulnerable turn 1-2, burst during buff turns.
+# Jaw Worm (hallway, Act 1, HP: 40-44)
+
+PATTERN: Always starts with Chomp (11 damage). After Chomp: 59% Bellow, 41% Thrash. After Bellow: 56% Thrash, 44% Chomp. After Thrash: 45% Bellow, 30% Thrash, 25% Chomp (if Thrash used twice in a row: 64% Bellow, 36% Chomp).
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Strength scaling from Bellow. Each Bellow gives +3 Str and 6 Block. Damage increases each turn the fight goes on.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Chomp: 11 damage (12 at A2)
+- Bellow: Gains 3 Strength + 6 Block (4 Str at A2, 5 Str + 9 Block at A17)
+- Thrash: 7 damage + 5 Block
+
+WHAT THIS MEANS: Turn 1 is always Chomp (11 damage) -- block for it. Turn 2 is likely Bellow (free turn, 0 damage). Kill fast before Strength accumulates. Apply Vulnerable turn 1-2, burst during Bellow turns.
BENCHMARKS: 4-turn kill typical. 10-17 damage per turn with Vulnerable.
BRING: Bash for Vulnerable, Strikes for follow-up.
AVOID: Slow decks that let Strength accumulate.
+
+DANGER LEVEL: Low. Standard Act 1 opener.
playbook/enemies/lagavulin.md
@@ -1,6 +1,14 @@
-# Lagavulin (elite, Act 1, HP: 110)
-PATTERN: Starts asleep with 8 block/turn. Wakes when attacked or after 3 turns. After waking: alternates between attack (18 damage) and debuff (-1 Strength, -1 Dexterity permanently).
-KEY MECHANIC: Permanent -1 Str/-1 Dex debuffs. These stack and never go away. Each debuff turn makes all your attacks and block cards permanently worse.
+# Lagavulin (elite, Act 1, HP: 109-111)
+
+PATTERN: Starts asleep with Metallicize 8 (gains 8 Block every turn). Wakes when it takes any damage (stunned for 1 turn, then attacks) or after 3 turns of no damage (immediately attacks). After waking, Metallicize is removed. Repeating cycle: Attack -> Attack -> Siphon Soul.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Attack: 18 damage (20 at A3)
+- Siphon Soul: Applies -1 Strength and -1 Dexterity permanently (-2 each at A18)
+
+HP: 109-111 at A0, 112-115 at A8
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Permanent -1 Str/-1 Dex debuffs from Siphon Soul. These stack and never go away. Each Siphon Soul makes all your attacks and block cards permanently worse.
WHAT THIS MEANS: Wake it immediately with Bash (Vulnerable > chipping through 8 block). Front-load damage on debuff turns (free turns). Kill before Strength debuffs accumulate. After 2+ debuff turns, recalculate all damage values: Strike goes from 6 to 5 to 4.
BENCHMARKS: 7-turn fight typical. Expect 20-36 HP damage. Use Weak Potion to reduce 18 to 13.
BRING: Bash+ for Vulnerable, Headbutt+ (top-deck Bash+ for Vulnerable refresh), burst damage, Weak source.
playbook/enemies/looter.md
@@ -1,9 +1,18 @@
-# Looter (hallway, Act 1, HP: ~44-48)
-PATTERN: Steals 15 gold per turn. Attacks for 10+ damage. May try to escape after stealing enough.
-KEY MECHANIC: Gold theft. Each turn it lives costs 15 gold.
-WHAT THIS MEANS: Kill fast to minimize gold loss. Prioritize over non-stealing enemies in multi-enemy fights.
-BENCHMARKS: Kill in 3-4 turns to limit theft.
+# Looter (hallway, Act 1, HP: 44-48)
+
+PATTERN: Turns 1-2: Mug (10 damage + steals gold). Turn 3+: 50% Lunge (12 damage) or 50% Smoke Bomb (6 Block). After Smoke Bomb, uses Escape next turn (flees with stolen gold, combat ends). If Lunge on Turn 3, Smoke Bomb follows on Turn 4, then Escape Turn 5.
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Thievery -- steals gold whenever it attacks. Stolen gold is returned if the Looter is killed. If it escapes, gold is lost permanently.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Mug: 10 damage (steals gold)
+- Lunge: 12 damage
+- Smoke Bomb: 0 damage, gains 6 Block
+- Escape: Flees combat with stolen gold
+
+WHAT THIS MEANS: Kill by Turn 3-4 to prevent escape and recover stolen gold. If Smoke Bomb intent appears, you have one turn left before it flees.
+BENCHMARKS: Kill in 3-4 turns to limit theft and prevent escape.
BRING: Burst damage.
-AVOID: Slow play (hemorrhages gold).
+AVOID: Slow play (hemorrhages gold and risks escape).
ACT 2 PAIRING: Looter appears alongside Mugger in an Act 2 hallway fight (Looter + Mugger). Combined damage is 20+ per turn with no free setup turns. This pairing punishes expensive setup cards (Corruption at 3E, Barricade at 3E) because you cannot afford a turn without block against two enemies attacking simultaneously. AOE and fast kills are critical.
playbook/enemies/louse.md
@@ -1,7 +1,23 @@
-# Louse (hallway, Act 1, HP: ~11-17, appears in groups of 2-3)
-PATTERN: Attacks for ~5-7 damage per turn. Has Curl Up passive (gains 5-7 block when first attacked).
-KEY MECHANIC: Curl Up absorbs first hit. In groups, combined damage adds up.
-WHAT THIS MEANS: Focus fire one at a time. The first attack triggers Curl Up block, so follow up with more attacks that turn.
+# Louse (hallway, Act 1, appears in groups of 2)
+
+Two types: Red Louse and Green Louse (the game calls Green Louse what the wiki calls "Green Louse" -- it applies Weak).
+
+## Red Louse — HP: 10-15
+- Bite: Deals N damage (N = random 5-7, set at combat start). 75% chance per turn.
+- Grow: Gains 3 Strength. 25% chance per turn.
+- Cannot use same move three times in a row.
+- Curl Up: Gains 3-7 Block when first hit by an attack.
+
+## Green Louse — HP: 11-17
+- Bite: Deals N damage (N = random 5-7, set at combat start). 75% chance per turn.
+- Spit Web: Applies 2 Weak. 25% chance per turn.
+- Cannot use same move three times in a row.
+- Curl Up: Gains 3-7 Block when first hit by an attack.
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Curl Up absorbs first hit. In groups, combined damage adds up. Red Louse's Grow (+3 Str) makes it scale if left alive.
+WHAT THIS MEANS: Focus fire one at a time. The first attack triggers Curl Up block, so follow up with more attacks that turn. Kill Red Louse first if it uses Grow (Strength scaling is more dangerous than Weak application).
BENCHMARKS: Kill one per turn with Vulnerable.
BRING: AOE if facing 3. Focus fire.
AVOID: Splitting damage across all Louses.
+
+DANGER LEVEL: Low. Standard Act 1 encounter.
playbook/enemies/mugger.md
@@ -1,8 +1,14 @@
-# Mugger (hallway, Act 2, HP: ~48-52)
+# Mugger (hallway, Act 2, HP: 48-52)
Act 2 version of the Looter. Steals gold and deals significant damage.
-PATTERN: Steals gold on some turns. Attacks for moderate damage (10-12+). May attempt to escape after stealing.
+PATTERN: Turns 1-2: Mug (10 damage + steals gold). Turn 3+: 50% Lunge (16 damage) or 50% Smoke Bomb (11 Block). After Smoke Bomb, uses Escape next turn (flees with stolen gold). Same sequence as Looter but higher damage and block values.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Mug: 10 damage (steals gold)
+- Lunge: 16 damage
+- Smoke Bomb: 0 damage, gains 11 Block
+- Escape: Flees combat with stolen gold
KEY MECHANIC: Gold theft, similar to Looter but appears in Act 2 where enemy damage is higher overall.
playbook/enemies/nemesis.md
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+# Nemesis (elite, Act 3, HP: 185)
+
+PATTERN: First turn has 50/50 chance of Scorch (Burns) or Attack. Subsequent turns: ~35% Scorch, ~35% Attack, ~30% Scythe. Cannot use Scorch or Scythe twice in a row. Cannot use Attack three times in a row. Every other turn starting from Turn 2, gains Intangible (all damage reduced to 1 for that turn).
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Intangible. On Intangible turns, ALL damage dealt to Nemesis is reduced to 1 -- including poison ticks, Thorns, everything. This means half your turns deal essentially zero damage. You must maximize damage on non-Intangible turns.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Attack (multi-hit): 6x3 = 18 damage (7x3 = 21 at A3)
+- Scythe: 45 damage (single massive hit)
+- Scorch: 0 damage, adds 3 Burns to discard pile (5 Burns at A18)
+
+DEBUFFS: Burns added to discard pile (Unplayable, deal 2 damage at end of turn if in hand)
+
+HP: 185 at A0, 200 at A8
+
+WHAT THIS MEANS: The fight has a rhythm -- non-Intangible turns are your damage windows, Intangible turns are for setup/blocking. Scythe (45 damage) is extremely dangerous and requires serious block (Impervious or Weak + multiple block cards). Burns accumulate and clog hand quality over a long fight.
+
+STRATEGY:
+- Track the Intangible pattern: Turn 1 (no Intangible), Turn 2 (Intangible), Turn 3 (no), Turn 4 (Intangible), etc.
+- On non-Intangible turns: deal maximum damage. Front-load burst here
+- On Intangible turns: do NOT waste damage cards. Use the turn for block, setup, Powers, or applying debuffs
+- Poison is partially countered -- each tick only deals 1 on Intangible turns. Poison still works but takes twice as long
+- Block for Scythe (45 damage). Impervious is ideal. Weak reduces it to 33
+- Burns management matters in long fights. Exhaust tools or kill speed to end before Burns overwhelm
+- Apply Weak to survive Scythe turns. Without Weak, 45 damage is near-unsurvivable
+
+SILENT-SPECIFIC: Poison is less efficient than usual due to Intangible halving effective poison damage (ticks reduced to 1 every other turn). High-burst poison (Catalyst+) on a non-Intangible turn is still strong. Wraith Form+ provides Intangible for YOU on the dangerous Scythe turns. Piercing Wail reduces the 6x3 multi-hit. Malaise reduces Strength for multiple turns. Well-Laid Plans+ can save burst damage for non-Intangible turns.
+
+DANGER LEVEL: High. Scythe (45 damage) can one-shot at low HP. Intangible doubles effective fight length. Burns clog draws.
+
+APPEARS WITH: Solo encounter.
playbook/enemies/orb-walker.md
@@ -1,13 +1,18 @@
-# Orb Walker (Act 3 Hallway, HP: ~93)
+# Orb Walker (Act 3 Hallway, HP: 90-96)
-Fast-scaling enemy. Gains +3 Strength per turn. Must burst down before damage becomes unsurvivable. Can appear as a single enemy or as a pair (2 Orb Walkers).
+Fast-scaling enemy. Gains +3 Strength at end of each turn (Strength Up 3). Must burst down before damage becomes unsurvivable. Can appear as a single enemy or as a pair (2 Orb Walkers).
-PATTERN:
-- Attacks every turn with increasing damage as Strength accumulates.
-- Turn 1: ~10 damage per Orb Walker (20 combined for 2).
-- Turn 2+: Damage increases by 3 per Orb Walker per turn (+3 Str per buff cycle).
-- Generates Burns that clog the hand and deal end-of-turn damage.
-- No free turns -- blocks or attacks every turn.
+PATTERN: Each turn: 60% Laser, 40% Claw. Cannot use either move 3x in a row.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Laser: 10 damage + adds 1 Burn to draw pile + 1 Burn to discard pile (11 at A2)
+- Claw: 15 damage (16 at A2)
+
+BUFFS: Strength Up 3 -- gains 3 Strength at end of each turn (5 at A17)
+
+HP: 90-96 at A0, 92-102 at A7
+
+Attacks every turn with increasing damage as Strength accumulates. Turn 1: 10-15 damage. Turn 2: 13-18. Turn 3: 16-21. Generates Burns that clog the hand and deal end-of-turn damage. No free turns.
KEY MECHANICS:
- **Strength scaling (+3/turn per Orb Walker)**: By Turn 3, a single OW attacks for 19+. With 2 Orb Walkers, combined incoming reaches 38+ by Turn 3 and 50+ by Turn 5. This makes the fight a DPS race.
playbook/enemies/red-slaver.md
@@ -1,7 +1,24 @@
-# Red Slaver (hallway, Act 1, HP: ~50)
-PATTERN: Attacks for 12-13 damage. Has debuff turns (Entangle or similar). Straightforward fight.
-KEY MECHANIC: Moderate damage per turn with occasional debuffs.
-WHAT THIS MEANS: Apply Vulnerable, burst down. 3-turn kill typical.
-BENCHMARKS: 3 turns with Vulnerable.
-BRING: Standard Bash + attacks.
-AVOID: Nothing special -- straightforward fight.
+# Red Slaver (hallway, Act 1, HP: 46-50)
+
+PATTERN: Always starts with Stab. Then repeats Scrape -> Scrape -> Stab until Entangle is used. Has 25% chance per turn to use Entangle instead of the normal pattern. After Entangle (used only once per combat): 55% Stab, 45% Scrape (cannot use same move 3x in a row).
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Entangle -- prevents playing ALL Attack cards for one turn. The only source of Entangled in the game. Can only be used once per combat.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Stab: 13 damage (14 at A2)
+- Scrape: 8 damage + 1 Vulnerable (9 + 1 Vuln at A2, 9 + 2 Vuln at A17)
+- Entangle: Applies Entangled (cannot play Attack cards this turn)
+
+DEBUFFS: Vulnerable (from Scrape), Entangled (from Entangle -- once per combat)
+
+WHAT THIS MEANS: Entangle can be devastating if it hits on a turn where you need to deal damage. After Entangle, you can only play Skills and Powers. Plan for this by having block Skills ready. Scrape's Vulnerable makes subsequent attacks hit harder.
+
+STRATEGY:
+- Apply Vulnerable, burst down. 3-4 turn kill typical
+- Block on Stab turns (13 damage)
+- When Entangled: play only Skills (Defend, block cards). Do NOT waste the turn -- use it for setup
+- Kill before Entangle if possible (25% chance per turn means it usually fires by turn 3-4)
+
+DANGER LEVEL: Low-Medium. Entangle is annoying but not lethal for decks with adequate block cards.
+
+APPEARS WITH: Solo in Act 1. With Blue Slaver + Taskmaster in Act 2 elite Slavers fight.
playbook/enemies/reptomancer.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Reptomancer (elite, Act 3, HP: 180-190)
+
+PATTERN: Always opens with Summon Dagger. Afterward uses approximately equal distribution of Snake Strike, Big Bite, and Summon Dagger. Cannot use Snake Strike or Big Bite twice in a row. Cannot use Summon Dagger three times in a row. If Summon Dagger is chosen with 4 Daggers already in play, uses Snake Strike instead. Maximum 4 Daggers active at any time.
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Dagger minions. Daggers are summoned with 20-25 HP and follow a fixed pattern: Stab (9 damage + adds Wound to discard) -> Explode (25 damage, Dagger dies). Each Dagger is a 2-turn time bomb. If not killed before Explode, it deals 25 damage. Multiple Daggers exploding on the same turn is lethal.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Big Bite: 30 damage (34 at A3)
+- Snake Strike: 13x2 = 26 damage + 1 Weak (16x2 = 32 at A3)
+- Dagger Stab: 9 damage + 1 Wound to discard
+- Dagger Explode: 25 damage (Dagger dies)
+
+HP: Reptomancer 180-190. Daggers 20-25 each.
+
+DEBUFFS: Weak from Snake Strike. Wound cards from Dagger Stab.
+
+COMBAT SETUP: Fight starts with Reptomancer + 2 Daggers (3 enemies total).
+
+WHAT THIS MEANS: This is one of the most dangerous fights in the game. The starting 2 Daggers will Stab Turn 1 (9 damage each + Wounds) then Explode Turn 2 (25 damage each = 50 total from Daggers alone). Meanwhile Reptomancer herself deals 26-30 damage. Total incoming Turn 2 can exceed 75 damage. You MUST kill Daggers before they Explode.
+
+STRATEGY:
+- KILL DAGGERS IMMEDIATELY. Each Dagger that explodes deals 25 damage. Kill them on their Stab turn before Explode
+- AOE is critical. Die Die Die, Blade Dance + Accuracy, Dagger Spray all hit multiple targets. Cards that damage all enemies are the highest priority
+- After clearing initial Daggers, focus damage on Reptomancer. She resummons, but reducing her HP means fewer total Dagger waves
+- Big Bite (30 damage) requires serious block. Track her pattern to anticipate Big Bite turns
+- Weak from Snake Strike reduces your damage -- re-apply Vulnerable to compensate
+
+SILENT-SPECIFIC: This is the Silent's best elite matchup in Act 3. Die Die Die+ (17x3 AOE) can kill all Daggers in one play. Blade Dance + Accuracy provides cheap multi-target damage. Piercing Wail reduces all enemy damage (critical for surviving multi-Dagger turns). Corpse Explosion turns killed Daggers into AOE damage against Reptomancer. Poison on Reptomancer ticks while you manage Daggers.
+
+KILL PRIORITY: Daggers about to Explode > Daggers that just Stabbed > Reptomancer
+
+DANGER LEVEL: Extreme. Combined burst from Dagger Explodes + Reptomancer attacks can deal 75+ in a single turn. Without AOE or fast Dagger removal, this fight ends runs.
+
+APPEARS WITH: Solo encounter (Reptomancer + her summoned Daggers).
playbook/enemies/repulsor.md
@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+# Repulsor (hallway, Act 3, HP: 29-35)
+
+PATTERN: Each turn, 80% chance to use Bash (adds 2 Dazed to draw pile), 20% chance to use Repulse (attack). Cannot use Repulse twice in a row.
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Dazed card generation. Dazed cards are Unplayable + Ethereal status cards that clog your draw pile. Each Bash adds 2 Dazed, rapidly degrading hand quality. Over 4 turns, 6-8 Dazed cards can be added, making it impossible to draw meaningful hands.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Repulse: 11 damage (13 at A2)
+
+DEBUFFS: Adds 2 Dazed cards to draw pile per Bash use
+
+WHAT THIS MEANS: Repulsors are not directly dangerous (low attack damage, low HP) but they degrade your deck quality, making OTHER enemies more dangerous. In multi-enemy fights, Repulsors make it harder to draw block and damage cards when you need them.
+
+STRATEGY:
+- Kill after Exploders but before Spikers in the 3 Shapes encounter
+- Low HP (29-35) means they die quickly to any focused damage
+- Evolve converts Dazed into card draws (if available)
+- The fight is a minor annoyance for strong decks but can cripple thin decks that rely on specific card draws
+
+SILENT-SPECIFIC: Dazed clogging is extra painful for the Silent since key combo pieces (Catalyst+, Wraith Form) need to be drawn at the right time. Tools of the Trade helps filter Dazed cards. Well-Laid Plans+ retains key cards across turns. Poison ignores deck clogging once applied.
+
+DANGER LEVEL: Low. Direct threat is minimal. Danger is indirect -- Dazed clogging makes companion enemies harder to deal with.
+
+APPEARS WITH: Other Shapes (Spikers, Exploders) in the 3 Shapes encounter.
playbook/enemies/sentry.md
@@ -1,6 +1,17 @@
-# Sentry (elite, Act 1, HP: ~40 each, appears as group of 3)
-PATTERN: Two Sentries attack (9 damage each = 18 total) while one adds Dazed status cards to deck. The attacking pair rotates each turn. Each starts with 1 Artifact. Each has Metallicize 4 (gains 4 block every turn automatically).
-KEY MECHANIC: Dazed card generation + Artifact protection + Metallicize 4. Dazed cards (Unplayable, Ethereal) clog draws. Artifact blocks first debuff application. Metallicize 4 means every Sentry gains 4 block/turn, requiring 4 extra damage to break through before HP damage.
+# Sentry (elite, Act 1, HP: 38-42 each, appears as group of 3)
+
+PATTERN: Outer two Sentries start with Bolt, middle Sentry starts with Beam. They alternate: Bolt -> Beam -> Bolt -> Beam.
+- Bolt: Adds 2 Dazed to discard pile (no damage)
+- Beam: 9 damage (10 at A2)
+
+Each Sentry starts with 1 Artifact.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Beam: 9 damage per Sentry (10 at A2). Two Sentries on Beam = 18-20 total.
+
+DEBUFFS: Dazed cards (Unplayable, Ethereal) added to discard via Bolt.
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Dazed card generation + Artifact protection. Dazed cards clog draws. Artifact blocks first debuff application per Sentry.
WHAT THIS MEANS: AOE is king -- Whirlwind for 15 damage to all 3 (45 total) is the best turn 1. Focus fire one at a time. Strip Artifact with Thunderclap before real debuffs. Kill before Dazed overwhelms draws.
**ELITE READINESS GATE:** Do NOT fight Sentries with a starter-quality deck (Strikes, Defends, Bash, 0-1 other cards). Without AOE or meaningful damage scaling, the fight takes 14-20 turns because: (1) Metallicize 4 absorbs 4 damage from every Strike, reducing effective damage to 2/hit; (2) Dazed clogging gives multiple turns with zero playable attack or block cards; (3) each Sentry kill takes 6-8 turns at 2 effective damage/hit. A 14-20 turn Sentry fight drains 52-56+ HP even with perfect blocking discipline. Two confirmed cases: (1) a deck with Strikes/Defends/Bash/Headbutt/SIO spent 20 turns and took 56 damage (76->20 HP); (2) a 10-card deck with Bash+/Shockwave/Limit Break but no AOE or Str scaling spent 14 turns and took 52 damage (60->8 HP) with Essence of Steel active. Even with Plated Armor 4 and correct play, the math is unsurvivable without damage scaling. The Sentries fight requires at least one of: AOE (Thunderclap, Whirlwind, Cleave), damage scaling (Inflame, Spot Weakness), or a strong attack card (Carnage, Pommel Strike+) to be winnable without catastrophic HP loss.
playbook/enemies/shelled-parasite.md
@@ -1,6 +1,15 @@
-# Shelled Parasite (hallway, Act 2, HP: 68-71)
-PATTERN: Attacks for 13+ damage per turn. Has Plated Armor 14 (blocks 14 damage per turn passively). Creates extremely long fights unless burst damage is available.
-KEY MECHANIC: Plated Armor 14 means the first 14 damage dealt each turn is absorbed. Only damage exceeding 14 actually reduces HP. This makes fights last 8-12+ turns, which is lethal at low HP.
+# Shelled Parasite (hallway, Act 2, HP: 68-72)
+
+PATTERN: Cannot use Fell on first turn. 40% Double Strike, 40% Life Suck, 20% Fell. Cannot use Double Strike or Life Suck 3x in a row. Cannot use Fell 2x in a row. At A17: always starts with Fell.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Double Strike: 6x2 = 12 damage (7x2 = 14 at A2)
+- Life Suck: 10 damage, heals equal to unblocked damage (12 at A2)
+- Fell: 18 damage + 2 Frail (21 at A2)
+
+BUFFS: Plated Armor 14 -- reduces incoming damage by 14 per turn (each attack that deals damage reduces Plated Armor by 1). If all Plated Armor is broken, Shelled Parasite is Stunned for one turn (happens once per combat).
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Plated Armor 14 absorbs damage from each attack. Each hit reduces Plated Armor by 1, regardless of damage dealt. Multi-hit attacks strip Plated Armor faster (Double Strike removes 2 stacks). Life Suck heals the Shelled Parasite for unblocked damage, creating an attrition problem.
WHAT THIS MEANS: Apply Vulnerable first, then use large single hits (not multiple small hits). Each hit must exceed 14 to make progress. Vulnerable reduces this effective threshold significantly.
BRING: Burst damage (Carnage, Heavy Blade), Vulnerable application (Bash, Uppercut). Strength scaling (Inflame, Strength Potion) helps every hit break through the armor threshold.
AVOID: Small multi-hit attacks (Whirlwind with low energy), decks that rely on chip damage, entering this fight below 40% HP.
playbook/enemies/slavers.md
@@ -1,10 +1,15 @@
-# Slavers (Elite, Act 2, 3 enemies: Slaver + Taskmaster + Slaver)
+# Slavers (Elite, Act 2, 3 enemies: Blue Slaver + Red Slaver + Taskmaster)
PATTERN: All three enemies attack on turn 1 for combined 27+ damage. No free setup turn. Subsequent turns continue with high combined damage from three independent attackers.
KEY MECHANIC: Three-enemy pressure from the very first turn. Unlike most multi-enemy fights, there is NO free turn -- all three attack immediately. Combined damage of 27+ per turn makes this fight extremely HP-dependent.
-HP: Slavers ~36-46 HP each (one observed at 36 HP with Preserved Insect -25% reduction, suggesting base ~48). Taskmaster ~60.
+HP:
+- Blue Slaver: 46-50 (Stab 12, Rake 7 + 1 Weak. 60% Stab / 40% Rake, no 3x same)
+- Red Slaver: 46-50 (Stab 13, Scrape 8 + 1 Vuln, Entangle once. Opens with Stab, then Scrape/Scrape/Stab until Entangle fires at 25% per turn)
+- Taskmaster: 54-60 (Scouring Whip: 7 damage + shuffles 1 Wound into discard pile)
+
+KILL PRIORITY: Red Slaver first (highest damage at 13 per Stab, applies Vulnerable which amplifies ALL incoming, Entangle can lock you out of attacks). Then Blue Slaver (Weak debuff is annoying but less dangerous than Vulnerable). Taskmaster last (only 7 damage and Wounds are manageable).
WHAT THIS MEANS: This fight is unwinnable at low HP. Entering at 21/77 HP (27%) resulted in death by turn 3 despite having Demon Form, Flame Barrier+, Rage, Disarm, Uppercut, and Rampage in the deck. The math is simple: 27+ incoming per turn, maximum ~21 block available per turn with good draws, means 6+ unblocked damage per turn. At 21 HP, that's 3-4 turns before death, and 3-4 turns is not enough to kill any of the three enemies.
playbook/enemies/small-slimes.md
@@ -0,0 +1,43 @@
+# Small Slimes (hallway, Act 1)
+
+Encounter consists of an Acid Slime (S) + Spike Slime (S), or variants with medium slimes. This is an Easy Pool encounter.
+
+## Acid Slime (S) — HP: 8-12
+
+MOVES:
+- Lick: Applies 1 Weak (no damage)
+- Tackle: 3 damage
+
+PATTERN: 50/50 between Lick and Tackle. Cannot use same move three times in a row.
+
+## Spike Slime (S) — HP: 10-14
+
+MOVES:
+- Tackle: 5 damage
+
+PATTERN: Uses Tackle every turn. No alternate moves.
+
+## Acid Slime (M) — HP: 28-32
+
+MOVES:
+- Corrosive Spit: 7 damage + 1 Slimed to discard
+- Lick: Applies 1 Weak
+- Tackle: 10 damage
+
+PATTERN: 30% Corrosive Spit, 40% Tackle, 30% Lick. Cannot use Tackle twice in a row; other moves cannot repeat three times in a row.
+
+## Spike Slime (M) — HP: 28-32
+
+MOVES:
+- Flame Tackle: 8 damage + 1 Slimed to discard
+- Lick: Applies 1 Frail
+
+PATTERN: 30% Flame Tackle, 70% Lick. Cannot use same move three times in a row.
+
+STRATEGY:
+- Trivial encounter. Small slimes have very low HP and damage
+- Kill the Acid Slime first to avoid Weak stacking
+- No special preparation needed
+- Good fight for preserving HP early in Act 1
+
+DANGER LEVEL: Low. One of the easiest encounters in the game.
playbook/enemies/snake-plant.md
@@ -1,6 +1,14 @@
-# Snake Plant (hallway, Act 2, HP: ~67-75)
-PATTERN: Attacks for 9x3=27 damage per turn (multi-attack). Has turns where it applies Frail and Weak as debuffs instead of attacking. Has Malleable mechanic. Can appear in Unknown rooms.
-KEY MECHANIC: **Malleable** -- gains increasing block each time it is attacked in a single turn. First hit in a turn: gains ~3 block. Second hit: more block. This punishes multi-hit strategies and rewards single large hits per turn. Also applies Frail (block reduced 25%) and Weak (damage reduced 25%), making the fight harder over time. The Frail + Weak combination with 9x3 multi-attacks is devastating: Frail cripples your block while 27 incoming overwhelms weakened defenses. This can drain 40+ HP in a single fight even from high HP.
+# Snake Plant (hallway, Act 2, HP: 75-79)
+
+PATTERN: 65% Chomp (7x3 = 21 damage), 35% Enfeebling Spores (applies 2 Frail + 2 Weak). Cannot use Chomp 3x in a row, cannot use Enfeebling Spores 2x in a row.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Chomp: 7x3 = 21 damage (8x3 = 24 at A2)
+- Enfeebling Spores: 0 damage, applies 2 Frail + 2 Weak
+
+BUFFS: Malleable 3 -- upon receiving attack damage, gains 3 Block (increases by 1 each time triggered in the same turn, resets at start of your turn).
+
+KEY MECHANIC: **Malleable 3** -- gains increasing block each time it is attacked in a single turn. First hit: gains 3 block. Second hit: 4 block. Third hit: 5 block. This punishes multi-hit strategies and rewards single large hits per turn. Also applies Frail (block reduced 25%) and Weak (damage reduced 25%), making the fight harder over time. The Frail + Weak combination with 7x3 multi-attacks is devastating: Frail cripples your block while 21-24 incoming overwhelms weakened defenses. This can drain 40+ HP in a single fight even from high HP.
WHAT THIS MEANS: Use single large attacks rather than many small hits per turn. Apply Vulnerable early. Clothesline/Uppercut for Weak reduces 9x3=27 to 6x3=18. Kill in 4-5 turns before debuffs stack. The debuff turn is a free turn for offense.
BRING: Burst damage (Immolate+, Carnage+, Rampage), Clothesline/Uppercut for Weak, single large hits. Bloodletting for extra energy on burst turns. **Strength scaling is critical** -- without Inflame or similar, even correct play (single large hits, Vulnerable applied) results in a 7-turn fight draining 40+ HP. With Strength scaling, the fight shortens to 4-5 turns and HP loss drops to 15-25.
AVOID: Multi-hit attacks which trigger Malleable block repeatedly. Whirlwind, Twin Strike, Sword Boomerang, and especially Pummel (4-5 hits at 2 base damage each) are the worst offenders. Pummel against Snake Plant generates 3+4+5+6 = 18 block from Malleable alone, making its 8-10 damage effectively zero. Two Snake Plant encounters with Whirlwind and Pummel in the deck can drain 40+ HP total if multi-hit cards are played carelessly. Use single large hits (Bludgeon, Carnage, Hemokinesis) instead. Entering at low HP -- 27 damage/turn with Frail making block weaker is a death spiral.
playbook/enemies/snecko.md
@@ -1,6 +1,13 @@
-# Snecko (hallway, Act 2, HP: ~114-120)
-PATTERN: Turn 1 applies Confusion debuff (randomizes card costs). Subsequent turns: alternates between attacks (13-22 damage) and debuff turns. Can apply Vulnerable to the player, making subsequent attacks deal 50% more damage. The Confusion + Vulnerable combo is Snecko's kill pattern.
-KEY MECHANIC: Confusion debuff. When applied, all cards in hand are randomly assigned costs of 0-3 each turn for the rest of combat. This can make expensive cards free or cheap cards unplayable. Persists until combat ends.
+# Snecko (hallway, Act 2, HP: 114-120)
+
+PATTERN: Always starts with Perplexing Glare (Confused debuff). Then each turn: 60% Bite, 40% Tail Whip. Cannot use Bite 3x in a row.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Bite: 15 damage (18 at A2)
+- Tail Whip: 8 damage + 2 Vulnerable (10 + 2 Vuln at A2; 10 + 2 Weak + 2 Vuln at A17)
+- Perplexing Glare: 0 damage, applies Confused
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Confusion debuff. When applied, all cards in hand are randomly assigned costs of 0-3 each turn for the rest of combat. This can make expensive cards free or cheap cards unplayable. Persists until combat ends. Tail Whip's Vulnerable makes subsequent Bite attacks hit for 22+ damage.
WHAT THIS MEANS: Kill fast before cost confusion ruins multiple turns. With good RNG, confused turns can be better than normal (0-cost Bash+, 0-cost Fiend Fire, 2-cost Demon Form). With bad RNG, entire turns are wasted on unplayable 3-cost cards. Apply Vulnerable early to maximize every successful attack turn. Metallicize is excellent here since Powers are played once (before or after Confusion) and provide guaranteed value every turn regardless of cost rolls. Demon Form is particularly strong: if drawn at reduced cost under Confusion, play it immediately for guaranteed scaling regardless of future cost rolls.
HP IS HIGH (~114-120): Despite being a hallway fight, Snecko is tanky. Plan for a 5-7 turn fight even with good damage.
BRING: Burst damage, Vulnerable, Powers (guaranteed value regardless of cost rolls), Metallicize (passive block every turn), Duplication Potion (duplicate a card that Confusion set to 0-cost for guaranteed free value), Gambler's Brew (redraws hand with new randomized costs -- effectively re-rolls Confusion RNG. Confirmed lifesaver at 7 HP when initial Confusion hand was unplayable).
playbook/enemies/spheric-guardian.md
@@ -1,5 +1,14 @@
-# Spheric Guardian (hallway, Act 2, HP: ~20, effectively 60+)
-PATTERN: Starts with 40 block + Barricade (block persists) + 3 Artifact + applies Frail 5. Alternates between attacking (10x2 = 20 damage) and defending (gains 15-20 block that persists due to Barricade). On attack turns, it deals 10x2=20. On defend turns, it deals 0 damage and adds block to its existing total.
+# Spheric Guardian (hallway, Act 2, HP: 20, effectively 60+)
+
+STARTS WITH: Artifact 3, Block 40, Barricade (block never expires).
+
+PATTERN: Fixed sequence: Activate (25 Block) -> Slam (10 damage + 5 Frail) -> then alternates Slam (10x2 = 20 damage) and Harden (10 damage + 15 Block) for rest of combat.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Activate: 0 damage, gains 25 Block
+- Slam (first): 10 damage + applies 5 Frail
+- Slam (subsequent): 10x2 = 20 damage
+- Harden: 10 damage + gains 15 Block
KEY MECHANIC: Barricade means block never expires. The Spheric Guardian GAINS block on defend turns (observed 15-20 block gained per action), and since block persists via Barricade, it stacks indefinitely. Combined with alternating attack/defend, the block grows faster than chip damage can remove it. At low Strength (0-1), Strikes deal 6-7 per play vs 15-20 block gained per defend cycle. The block will grow every other turn even while being attacked on the off turns.
WHAT THIS MEANS: This is a DPS check disguised as a hallway fight. At 1 Strength, Strikes deal 6-7 damage per play. The Spheric Guardian gains 15-20 block on defend turns and retains leftover block. After 5-6 turns, its block reaches 40-64 and never comes down. The fight becomes mathematically unwinnable without sufficient Strength scaling or burst damage. Strip 3 Artifact charges first with cheap debuffs (Thunderclap ideal). Then apply Vulnerable. Then burst through remaining block + HP.
BENCHMARKS: Must kill within 6-8 turns or block becomes insurmountable. Expect 30-50+ HP lost from Frail-reduced blocking vs 10x2 attacks. At low Strength without burst, this fight is a guaranteed loss through attrition.
playbook/enemies/spike-slime-l.md
@@ -1,6 +1,15 @@
-# Spike Slime L (hallway/boss split, Act 1, HP: equal to Slime Boss's remaining HP at split, max 70)
-PATTERN: Attacks for ~16 damage. Applies Frail. Adds Slimed cards. Splits into 2x Spike Slime (M) on death.
-KEY MECHANIC: Split on death. Creates 2 medium slimes, extending the fight significantly. Frail reduces block gained from cards by 25%.
-WHAT THIS MEANS: Prepare for the split -- have enough HP and resources to handle 2 medium slimes after killing the large one. In the Slime Boss fight, killing this while Acid Slime (L) is at full HP creates a 3-enemy nightmare.
+# Spike Slime L (hallway/boss split, Act 1, HP: 64-70 standalone, or equal to Slime Boss's remaining HP at split)
+
+PATTERN: 30% Flame Tackle, 70% Lick. Cannot use same move three times in a row. Splits into 2x Spike Slime (M) when HP drops to 50% or below.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Flame Tackle: 16 damage + 2 Slimed cards to discard pile (18 at A2)
+- Lick: Applies 2 Frail (no damage)
+- Split: At 50% HP or below, spawns 2 Spike Slime (M) with current HP each
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Split at 50% HP. Creates 2 medium slimes with current HP, extending the fight significantly. Frail 2 reduces block gained from cards by 25%.
+WHAT THIS MEANS: Prepare for the split -- have enough HP and resources to handle 2 medium slimes after the split. In the Slime Boss fight, killing this while Acid Slime (L) is at full HP creates a 3-enemy nightmare.
BRING: AOE for post-split. Exhaust for Slimed.
AVOID: Long attrition fights (8+ turns drain HP badly). Killing this before softening the other large slime.
+
+DANGER LEVEL: Medium.
playbook/enemies/spike-slime-m.md
@@ -1,7 +1,15 @@
-# Spike Slime M (hallway, Act 1, HP: ~28)
-PATTERN: Attacks for ~8 damage. Has debuff turns (applies Frail instead of attacking). Adds Slimed status cards to deck.
-KEY MECHANIC: Frail + Slimed card generation.
-WHAT THIS MEANS: Exhaust Slimed cards when possible. Block is reduced by Frail.
+# Spike Slime M (hallway, Act 1, HP: 28-32)
+
+PATTERN: 30% Flame Tackle, 70% Lick. Cannot use same move three times in a row.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Flame Tackle: 8 damage + 1 Slimed card to discard pile (10 at A2)
+- Lick: Applies 1 Frail (no damage)
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Frail (reduces block by 25%) + Slimed card generation (clogs deck).
+WHAT THIS MEANS: Mostly uses Lick (70% of turns), so blocking needs are moderate. Exhaust Slimed cards when possible. Frail reduces block effectiveness.
BENCHMARKS: Standard fight. Kill in 3-4 turns.
BRING: Exhaust tools for Slimed cards.
AVOID: Long fights where Slimed cards accumulate.
+
+DANGER LEVEL: Low.
playbook/enemies/spiker.md
@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+# Spiker (hallway, Act 3, HP: 42-56)
+
+PATTERN: Each turn, 50/50 chance of using Cut (attack) or Spike (buff Thorns). Cannot use Cut twice in a row. Cannot use Spike after it has been used 6 times (forces Cut instead).
+
+KEY MECHANIC: Thorns. Starts with Thorns 3. Each Spike adds +2 Thorns. Every time you deal attack damage to a Spiker, you take damage equal to its current Thorns. As Thorns stack (3 -> 5 -> 7 -> 9 -> 11 -> 13 -> 15), attacking becomes increasingly costly. After 6 Spikes, Thorns reach 15 -- every attack card played costs 15 HP.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Cut: 7 damage (9 at A2)
+- Thorns retaliation: starts at 3 per attack hit, grows by 2 per Spike turn
+
+BUFFS: Thorns 3 (innate), gains +2 Thorns per Spike use
+
+WHAT THIS MEANS: Poison is the ideal damage source -- it bypasses Thorns entirely. Multi-hit attacks (Blade Dance, Dagger Spray) are extremely punished since each hit triggers Thorns separately. A 4-hit Blade Dance against Thorns 9 costs 36 HP. Single large attacks are better than many small ones if you must use attacks. Kill Spikers before Thorns stack too high.
+
+STRATEGY:
+- Poison is king. Noxious Fumes, Deadly Poison, Bouncing Flask all deal damage without triggering Thorns
+- If you must attack, use single large hits (Dash+, Predator) not multi-hit attacks
+- Kill Spikers early before Thorns compound. At Thorns 3-5, attacks are tolerable. At 9+, every attack card is a significant HP loss
+- In multi-enemy fights (3 Shapes), kill the Spiker first to stop Thorns from scaling
+
+SILENT-SPECIFIC: Poison completely solves Spikers. Noxious Fumes + any poison source kills without ever taking Thorns damage. Shivs and Blade Dance are TRAPS -- each hit triggers Thorns. If Shivs are the only damage source, accept the Thorns cost but prioritize killing Spikers fast. Piercing Wail does nothing useful here (Spikers' Cut damage is low; Thorns is the real threat).
+
+DANGER LEVEL: Medium. Low direct damage but the Thorns mechanic punishes attack-heavy decks severely. Poison decks trivialize this fight.
+
+APPEARS WITH: Other Shapes (Exploders, Repulsors) in the 3 Shapes encounter. Can also appear in mixed Act 3 hallway fights.
playbook/enemies/the-maw.md
@@ -1,11 +1,18 @@
-# The Maw (Act 3 Hallway, HP: ~300)
+# The Maw (Act 3 Hallway, HP: 300)
-Tanky single-target enemy with STRONG_DEBUFF on Turn 1 and escalating damage. Long fight (8-9 turns) that rewards defensive setup over burst.
+Tanky single-target enemy with Roar on Turn 1 and escalating damage. Long fight (8-9 turns) that rewards defensive setup over burst. HP does NOT change with Ascension.
PATTERN:
-- **Turn 1**: STRONG_DEBUFF (Weak + Frail + Vulnerable applied to player). No damage.
-- **Turns 2+**: Alternates between BUFF (free turns, no damage) and ATTACK. Damage escalates: ~5 -> 16 -> 33 -> 56+.
-- Multiple free turns (BUFF intent) allow Power setup without HP loss.
+- **Turn 1**: Roar (applies 3 Weak + 3 Frail). No damage.
+- **Turns 2+**: After Drool -> 50/50 Slam or Nom Nom. After Slam -> 50/50 Nom Nom or Drool. After Nom Nom -> always Drool.
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Roar: 0 damage, applies 3 Weak + 3 Frail
+- Slam: 25 damage (30 at A2)
+- Nom Nom: 5xN damage (N increases -- roughly current turn / 2 rounded up)
+- Drool: 0 damage, gains 3 Strength (5 at A5)
+
+Multiple free turns (Drool and Roar intents) allow Power setup without HP loss.
KEY MECHANICS:
- **STRONG_DEBUFF Turn 1**: Applies Weak, Frail, and Vulnerable simultaneously. Frail reduces block by 25%, Weak reduces attack by 25%, Vulnerable increases damage taken by 50%. Lasts 2-3 turns. Plan to block through Frail-reduced values.
playbook/enemies/transient.md
@@ -2,14 +2,17 @@
The Transient disappears after 5 turns (Fading 5 loses 1 per turn, dies at 0). The fight is a pure survival check -- outlast 5 turns of escalating damage.
-PATTERN:
-- **Turn 1**: Attacks for 30 damage.
-- **Turn 2**: Attacks for 40 damage.
-- **Turn 3**: Attacks for 50 damage.
-- **Turn 4**: Attacks for 60 damage.
-- **Turn 5**: Attacks for 70 damage.
-- **Total damage over 5 turns: 250.** Damage increases by 10 per turn.
-- **Shifting**: Gains block when attacked. Attacking the Transient is generally wasteful -- it has 999 HP and cannot be killed through damage in practice.
+PATTERN: Attacks every turn with escalating damage.
+- **Turn 1**: 30 damage (40 at A2)
+- **Turn 2**: 40 damage (50 at A2)
+- **Turn 3**: 50 damage (60 at A2)
+- **Turn 4**: 60 damage (70 at A2)
+- **Turn 5**: 70 damage (80 at A2)
+- **Total damage over 5 turns: 250 at A0.** Damage increases by 10 per turn.
+
+POWERS:
+- **Fading 5**: Loses 1 per turn, dies at 0 (6 at A17)
+- **Shifting**: Upon losing HP, loses that much Strength until end of turn. This means dealing damage DOES reduce its attack for that turn -- but the Strength resets next turn. Attacking is NOT pointless if you need to reduce incoming damage this turn.
KEY MECHANICS:
- **Fading 5**: The Transient loses 1 Fading per turn and dies when Fading reaches 0. You do NOT need to deal 999 damage. Just survive 5 turns. **CRITICAL: The Transient attacks BEFORE it fades.** On Fading 1 (the last turn), the Transient delivers its final attack and then vanishes at end of turn. You must survive this last attack -- you cannot "almost" outlast it.
playbook/enemies/writhing-mass.md
@@ -1,10 +1,18 @@
-# Writhing Mass (Act 3 Hallway, HP: ~160)
+# Writhing Mass (Act 3 Hallway, HP: 160)
-A tanky enemy with two synergistic defensive mechanics that make it a significant HP drain even with a strong deck.
+A tanky enemy with two synergistic defensive mechanics that make it a significant HP drain even with a strong deck. HP increases to 175 at A7.
PATTERN:
-- Alternates between attacks (single-hit and multi-hit) and debuff turns (STRONG_DEBUFF applying Vulnerable, Frail, Weakened).
-- Multi-hit attacks (e.g., 4x3, 6x3) are the primary damage source.
+- First turn: equal random chance among Multi Hit, Big Hit, or Debuff Attack
+- Subsequent turns: 30% Multi Hit, 10% Big Hit, 20% Debuff Attack, 30% Block Attack, 10% Parasite
+- Parasite can only be used once. Same move cannot be used (or rerolled via Reactive) twice in a row
+
+DAMAGE:
+- Multi Hit: 7x3 = 21 damage (9x3 = 27 at A2)
+- Big Hit: 32 damage (38 at A2)
+- Debuff Attack: 10 damage + 2 Weak + 2 Vulnerable (12 at A2)
+- Block Attack: 15 damage + gains 16 Block (16 at A2)
+- Parasite: Permanently adds Parasite curse to your deck (once per combat)
KEY MECHANICS:
- **Reactive**: Changes the Writhing Mass's weakness/intent when you play certain card types. Unpredictable -- you cannot reliably plan around its intent shifts.
playbook/strategy.md
@@ -2,20 +2,21 @@
High-level strategic principles for Ironclad runs.
-**CURRENT STATUS (155 runs, 3 WINS):** Three Ironclad victories in four runs (Runs 147, 148, 150). Five Silent runs (Runs 151-155). Run 151 reached Awakened One (Act 3 boss), died Phase 2. Run 152 reached The Collector (Act 2 boss), died Floor 33. Run 153 reached Floor 45 (best Silent floor), died to Transient with Mark of the Bloom preventing recovery from Orb Walker fight. Run 154 died to Hexaghost (Act 1 boss) at Floor 16 -- defensive-heavy deck with Dex 6 from 2x Footwork+ blocked perfectly but had insufficient damage output (23-turn fight, Burns overwhelmed). Run 155 died to Book of Stabbing (Act 2 elite) at Floor 23 -- entered at 47% HP with no potions, no exhaust tools. Dex stacking + Shiv damage dealt 137 of 161 HP but Book's +3 Str/turn overwhelmed defenses by Turn 6. TWO proven Ironclad winning formulas exist: (1) Snecko Eye + Immolate+ + Limit Break engine, and (2) Barricade + Corruption + Feel No Pain + Entrench + Body Slam engine. Silent strategy is developing -- poison engine dominated Act 3 hallway fights, card retention engine (WLP+ + TotT+ + Tactician+) was excellent, but Mind Bloom "I am Awake" choice (Mark of the Bloom) and fighting Mysterious Sphere before the boss were critical strategic errors. Hexaghost punishes the Silent's lack of Burns management -- kill speed through poison scaling (Deadly Poison + Catalyst+) is mandatory. Book of Stabbing punishes the Silent's lack of exhaust tools -- Dex stacking alone cannot keep pace with +3 Str/turn.
+**CURRENT STATUS (156 runs, 3 WINS):** Three Ironclad victories in four runs (Runs 147, 148, 150). Six Silent runs (Runs 151-156). Run 151 reached Awakened One (Act 3 boss), died Phase 2. Run 152 reached The Collector (Act 2 boss), died Floor 33. Run 153 reached Floor 45 (best Silent floor), died to Transient with Mark of the Bloom preventing recovery from Orb Walker fight. Run 154 died to Hexaghost (Act 1 boss) at Floor 16 -- defensive-heavy deck with Dex 6 from 2x Footwork+ blocked perfectly but had insufficient damage output (23-turn fight, Burns overwhelmed). Run 155 died to Book of Stabbing (Act 2 elite) at Floor 23 -- entered at 47% HP with no potions, no exhaust tools. Run 156 died to The Collector (Act 2 boss) at Floor 33 -- Wraith Form played too early in a 19-turn fight caused -17 Dex by endgame, making all Skill block 0. Collector at 5/282 HP when player died (5 HP short). TWO proven Ironclad winning formulas exist: (1) Snecko Eye + Immolate+ + Limit Break engine, and (2) Barricade + Corruption + Feel No Pain + Entrench + Body Slam engine. Silent strategy is developing -- Wraith Form timing is the critical lesson from Run 156: in 15+ turn boss fights, delay Wraith Form until the final 5-6 turns. Poison engine + After Image + Blur is the defensive package for long Collector fights. The Collector's DEFEND_BUFF +3 Str scaling makes long fights exponentially more dangerous.
**SCORECARD (runs 101-155):**
- Act 1 boss wins: ~73%. All three Act 1 bosses beaten across runs.
- Act 2 boss kills: 4. Act 2 boss reaches: 9+.
- **Act 3 reached six times (Runs 145, 147, 148, 149, 150, 151).** Run 145 died to Transient. Run 149 died to Giant Head elite. Run 151 (Silent) died to Awakened One Phase 2 at Floor 50. Runs 147, 148, 150 all WON.
- **Best floor: 51 -- VICTORY (Runs 147, 148, 150 all reached Floor 51).**
-- **3 wins in 155 runs (1.9% win rate).** Ascension 0. Three Ironclad wins. Zero Silent wins (5 attempts: Run 151 reached Act 3 boss, Run 152 reached Act 2 boss, Run 153 reached Floor 45, Run 154 died Act 1 boss Floor 16, Run 155 died Act 2 elite Floor 23).
+- **3 wins in 156 runs (1.9% win rate).** Ascension 0. Three Ironclad wins. Zero Silent wins (6 attempts: Run 151 reached Act 3 boss, Run 152 reached Act 2 boss, Run 153 reached Floor 45, Run 154 died Act 1 boss Floor 16, Run 155 died Act 2 elite Floor 23, Run 156 reached Act 2 boss Floor 33).
- **No UI misplays in Runs 147-151.** Nine+ consecutive clean runs.
- **Mechanics error in Run 151: Awakened One Curiosity mechanic ignored.** Player played 6 Powers into a boss that gains +2 Str per Power. This was a fundamental boss mechanic misunderstanding, not an arithmetic error. Awakened One playbook entry created.
- **Run 152: Silent died to Collector at F33.** Defensive powers (After Image, Footwork+) not played early enough. Card index confusion on Turn 1 caused suboptimal command execution. Post-debuff incoming was unsurvivable without defensive powers active. Poison strategy was highly effective against block-based enemies (Spheric Guardian crushed).
- **Run 153: Silent died to Transient at F45.** Best Silent floor yet. Strong poison engine + card retention engine dominated Act 3 hallways. Two critical strategic errors: (1) Mind Bloom "I am Awake" gave Mark of the Bloom (no healing), then (2) Mysterious Sphere fight (2 Orb Walkers) drained 66 HP with no recovery possible. Entered Transient at 3 HP. Died 7 damage short of surviving the final turn.
- **Run 154: Silent died to Hexaghost at F16.** Defensive-heavy deck with 2x Footwork+ (Dex 6), Dash+, Masterful Stab. Took zero damage in 5/6 pre-boss fights. But damage output was only ~10/turn average -- fight lasted 23 turns, Burns accumulated to 12+ per turn, and second Inferno (8x6=48) hit with a hand of all Burns. Hexaghost at 24/250 HP when killed. The problem was entirely damage, not defense.
- **Run 155: Silent died to Book of Stabbing at F23.** Dex 6 + Accuracy Shiv deck with Piercing Wail, TotT, Sacred Bark. Beat Slime Boss near-zero damage. Act 2 Sentry+Spheric Guardian drained 26 HP (59->33). Entered Book of Stabbing at 47% HP with zero potions. Dealt 137/161 damage but Book's +3 Str/turn overwhelmed Dex block by Turn 6. Died with 24 HP remaining on Book.
+- **Run 156: Silent died to Collector at F33.** Wraith Form played too early in a 19-turn fight. Dex dropped to -17 by Turn 19, making all Skill-based block 0. After Image provided ~7-8 block/turn (only block source at negative Dex) but insufficient against Collector's Str-scaled attacks (Str 14+ from repeated DEFEND_BUFF). Collector at 5/282 HP when player died -- 5 HP short. Blur carried block from Intangible turns but was insufficient to bridge the full Dex gap. Key lesson: Wraith Form must be delayed until the final 5-6 turns in boss fights expected to last 15+ turns. Created wraith-form.md, after-image.md, blur.md.
- **Two distinct Ironclad winning formulas confirmed.** See THE WINNING FORMULAS below.
**THE WINNING FORMULAS (confirmed by 3 victories across 2 distinct engines):**
@@ -339,8 +340,9 @@ If you have 0-1 of these, the boss will likely kill you. Adjust card picks in re
- AOE damage (HIGHEST PRIORITY) -- Immolate+ is the single best card. Hits Collector + both Torch Heads. With Vulnerable: 42 damage to Collector per cast. Torch Heads respawn, so AOE is more valuable than single-target.
- Mass debuff -- Shockwave+ (Weak 3 + Vuln 3 to ALL enemies) reduces combined incoming by 25% while boosting all damage by 50%. Second-highest priority.
- Impervious -- 30 block absorbs post-debuff turns. Critical defensive card.
-- Disarm -- permanent Str reduction compounds over the 10-turn fight. Play Turn 1.
+- Disarm -- permanent Str reduction compounds over the 10-turn fight. Play Turn 1. Especially important because DEFEND_BUFF gives the Collector +3 Str per use -- Disarm partially offsets this scaling.
- Block density for post-debuff turns -- Frail reduces block by 25%, need multiple sources
+- Kill speed matters -- DEFEND_BUFF gives ALL enemies +3 Str per use. In fights lasting 15+ turns, the Collector scales from Str 3 to Str 14+, making late-fight attacks devastating. Do not allow the fight to drag.
**Bronze Automaton needs:**
- HP entry at 70%+ or Pantograph -- 300 HP boss with +3 Str/cycle scaling. HP alone is insufficient without block scaling. Block alone is insufficient without HP. Both are required.
Run 155 analysis: Silent F22 Book of Stabbing, Spheric Guardian mode shift
