playbook/cards/intimidate.md
@@ -1,7 +1,8 @@
-# Intimidate (0E, Skill, apply 1 Weak to ALL enemies, Exhaust)
+# Intimidate (0E, Skill, apply 1 Weak to ALL enemies, Exhaust | +: apply 2 Weak to ALL enemies, Exhaust)
Free mass Weak. Zero downside.
-WHEN IT'S GOOD: ALWAYS play when drawn. 0 energy cost means it never competes with other cards. Reduces all enemy attack damage by 25% for 1 turn.
+WHEN IT'S GOOD: ALWAYS play when drawn. 0 energy cost means it never competes with other cards. Reduces all enemy attack damage by 25% for 1 turn (2 turns upgraded).
DECISION POINTS: Play immediately on draw. There is zero reason to hold this card. Against Gremlin Nob and Chosen, do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage/Hex).
-SYNERGIES: Letter Opener (every Skill played deals 5 damage -- Intimidate is free 5 damage + mass Weak).
-MATCHUPS: Guardian -- Weak reduces 32-damage attack to 24. Multi-enemy fights -- affects all enemies for free.
+UPGRADE: 1 Weak -> 2 Weak. Doubles the duration of damage reduction. Worth upgrading when the deck has few other debuff sources.
+SYNERGIES: Letter Opener (every Skill played deals 5 damage -- Intimidate is free 5 damage + mass Weak). Corruption (Intimidate becomes free AND exhausts, which it already does -- under Corruption there is zero cost). Feel No Pain (exhaust triggers 3 block).
+MATCHUPS: Guardian -- Weak reduces 32-damage attack to 24. Multi-enemy fights -- affects all enemies for free. Gremlin Leader -- mass Weak on all gremlins + Leader reduces Rally-boosted damage significantly. Spheric Guardian -- contributes to debuff strategy after Artifact is stripped.
playbook/enemies/gremlin-leader.md
@@ -1,7 +1,9 @@
# Gremlin Leader (elite, Act 2, HP: ~131)
PATTERN: Summons Sneaky Gremlins (~10-12 HP each). Uses DEFEND_BUFF (Rally): gives ALL enemies +3 Strength permanently and stacking. Gremlins attack for ~9 base + accumulated Str. Leader itself attacks for significant damage when not rallying. Leader re-summons gremlins when they die -- killing gremlins is temporary relief, not permanent removal.
-KEY MECHANIC: Rally gives +3 Str to ALL enemies (Leader + all gremlins). Permanent and stacking. After 2 rallies: every enemy has +6 Str. The fight's damage scales exponentially with turn count. With +3 Str after one rally, gremlins deal 9+3=12 each. With two gremlins attacking, that is 24 damage from gremlins alone, plus the Leader's own attacks. Second data point: at 54% HP entry (43/80), turn 3 total enemy damage was 44 (mathematically unblockable with a 19-card deck containing 5 Strikes). Even with FNP + Shockwave+ + Fiend Fire+ burst tools, the fight ended in death because gremlins + Leader combined output exceeded all available block. The fight is unwinnable at low HP because there is no time to set up before Rally scaling makes incoming damage lethal.
-WHAT THIS MEANS: KILL GREMLINS FAST to reduce per-turn damage, but prioritize Leader damage when possible because gremlins get re-summoned. AOE is critical for clearing gremlins efficiently. Apply Shockwave early for mass Weak + Vulnerable. The fight becomes mathematically unwinnable once Strength stacks too high (by turn 3-4 with multiple gremlins alive, incoming damage is 40+).
-BENCHMARKS: Must win by turn 5-6 or Rally scaling overwhelms you. Enter with 60%+ HP minimum -- 54% (43/80) was insufficient even with strong burst tools. At 25% HP (14/56), the fight is unwinnable even with Nilry's Codex generating Impervious and Immolate. Bloated decks (19 cards with 5 Strikes) cannot draw answers fast enough against Rally's exponential scaling.
-BRING: AOE (Thunderclap, Whirlwind, Immolate+), Shockwave+ (mass debuff), Disarm (reduce Leader Str). Non-exhaust damage cards for sustained fighting. Rage (0E power that generates block per attack -- excellent value in multi-attack turns). Potions -- this fight demands aggressive potion use.
-AVOID: Entering below 60% HP. Exhaust-heavy decks (once Impervious/Shockwave/Disarm are gone, remaining deck is too weak). If below 50% HP, take any alternative path. Decks with reduced Max HP (e.g., post-Vampires at 56 Max HP) have no margin for error against this fight's scaling.
+KEY MECHANIC: Rally gives +3 Str to ALL enemies (Leader + all gremlins). Permanent and stacking. After 2 rallies: every enemy has +6 Str. The fight's damage scales exponentially with turn count. With +3 Str after one rally, gremlins deal 9+3=12 each. With two gremlins attacking, that is 24 damage from gremlins alone, plus the Leader's own attacks.
+RALLY SCALING MATH: Rally fires roughly every other turn. By turn 3, all enemies have +3 Str. By turn 5, all enemies have +6 Str. By turn 7, all enemies have +9 Str. With 2 gremlins alive at +9 Str, gremlins alone deal (9+9)*2 = 36, plus the Leader's own attacks. Total incoming by turn 7-8 exceeds 50 per turn. Every wasted turn is effectively +3 Str to the entire board -- a single lost turn (e.g., ending turn early after Battle Trance draw without playing drawn cards) can be the difference between winning and losing.
+WHAT THIS MEANS: TURN ECONOMY IS EVERYTHING. Every turn must deal maximum damage or apply critical debuffs. Wasting even one turn (misplaying end-of-turn, failing to use drawn cards) allows Rally to stack once more, which compounds across all gremlins. KILL GREMLINS FAST to reduce per-turn damage, but prioritize Leader damage when possible because gremlins get re-summoned. AOE is critical for clearing gremlins efficiently. Apply Shockwave early for mass Weak + Vulnerable. The fight becomes mathematically unwinnable once Strength stacks too high (by turn 3-4 with multiple gremlins alive, incoming damage is 40+).
+BENCHMARKS: Must win by turn 5-6 or Rally scaling overwhelms you. Enter with 60%+ HP minimum -- 56% (56/98) was insufficient even with Corruption + Feel No Pain engine and strong burst tools. At 25% HP (14/56), the fight is unwinnable even with Nilry's Codex generating Impervious and Immolate. Bloated decks (19 cards with 5 Strikes) cannot draw answers fast enough against Rally's exponential scaling.
+BRING: AOE (Thunderclap, Whirlwind, Immolate+) for gremlin clearing, Shockwave+ (mass debuff), Disarm (reduce Leader Str). Non-exhaust damage cards for sustained fighting. Rage (0E power that generates block per attack -- excellent value in multi-attack turns). Potions -- this fight demands aggressive potion use. Fairy in a Bottle as safety net (but expect it to be consumed early if the fight goes long -- a Fairy consumed on turn 6 leaves no safety for turns 7-8 when Rally scaling peaks).
+CORRUPTION + FNP ENGINE: Corruption makes Skills free, Feel No Pain generates block per exhaust. This engine is theoretically strong here but ONLY if it comes online by turn 2-3. If the engine activates on turn 7 of 8, Rally has already stacked to +9 Str on all enemies and incoming damage exceeds all available block. The engine needs early activation or it arrives too late.
+AVOID: Entering below 60% HP. Exhaust-heavy decks (once Impervious/Shockwave/Disarm are gone, remaining deck is too weak). If below 50% HP, take any alternative path. Decks with reduced Max HP (e.g., post-Vampires at 56 Max HP) have no margin for error against this fight's scaling. Wasting turns -- do NOT batch `end` with draw effects like Battle Trance; the drawn cards need to be played that same turn.
playbook/enemies/spheric-guardian.md
@@ -14,4 +14,6 @@ FRAIL 5 INTERACTION: Frail 5 reduces block from ALL sources by 25%. Defend gives
DEMON FORM SCALING: Even Demon Form scaling to 13 Strength by turn 9 was insufficient to kill Spheric Guardian through 65+ Barricade block. The fight lasted 9 turns and still ended in a loss. Demon Form is too slow -- by the time Strength ramps, block is already insurmountable. Front-loaded Strength (Spot Weakness, Inflame, Limit Break) or burst damage is required.
+FIRST SURVIVAL: Successfully defeated Spheric Guardian with only 12 HP lost. The winning formula was Corruption + Feel No Pain engine with Shockwave+ for debuffs and strong burst damage (Double Tap, Intimidate). The Corruption engine made Skills free, Feel No Pain generated block per exhaust, and front-loaded damage broke through Barricade block before it could stack. This confirms the playbook guidance: front-loaded Strength and burst damage (not Demon Form slow ramp) is the answer.
+
WARNING: This is the single most dangerous Act 2 hallway fight. Four deaths confirmed against this enemy (at 26%, 43%, 47%, and 100% HP entry). The fourth death (47% HP, 38/80) involved Demon Form + Metallicize + Spot Weakness scaling that reached 13 Str by turn 9, with Uppercut+ stripping Artifact charges -- and still lost. Zero potions available. Frail 5 crippled blocking during the critical early turns while Demon Form was ramping. Barricade block reached 65+ and grew faster than 13 Str could deplete it. The third death occurred at FULL HP (87/87) in a Sentry + Spheric Guardian pairing from an Unknown room. Despite having Barricade (player card), Rage+, Rampage+, Blood for Blood, and Stone Calendar, the fight was still lost because: (1) Brutality's 1 HP/turn self-damage ticked HP from 9 to 1 over the long fight, making survival impossible, (2) Rage+ expires at end of turn (not permanent), so the player had no block generation on the critical final turn, and (3) Frail 5 reduced block cards by 25%, making even True Grit+ insufficient. Even entering at full HP, the fight drained 86 HP over 6 turns through a combination of Spheric Guardian attacks, Sentry attacks, and Brutality self-damage. Reaper is useless in this fight (all damage absorbed by block = zero healing). Brutality is actively harmful (extends the death clock). Potions are critical for this fight -- entering with zero potions removes the only way to supplement insufficient scaling. Only massive single-turn burst or sustained Strength scaling that comes ONLINE FAST (Inflame, Spot Weakness, not Demon Form) can win.
playbook/potions/fairy-in-a-bottle.md
@@ -2,3 +2,4 @@
EFFECT: When HP reaches 0, automatically revive with ~30% Max HP. Triggers automatically, no manual use needed.
OPTIMAL TIMING: Keep as safety net for boss fights or dangerous elites. Triggers when you would die, reviving at ~30% Max HP. WARNING: Fairy is often consumed in Centurion+Mystic hallway fights. If possible, save it for elites/bosses -- don't waste it in hallway fights.
NOTE: This is a one-time safety net. Once consumed, it's gone. Plan paths so Fairy is available for the most dangerous fight, not consumed early.
+GREMLIN LEADER WARNING: In scaling fights like Gremlin Leader, Fairy can be consumed mid-fight (e.g., turn 6 of an 8-turn fight) when Rally-boosted gremlins spike damage past your remaining HP. Once consumed, there is no safety net for the final turns when Rally scaling peaks (+9 Str to all enemies). Fairy buys one extra turn but does NOT solve the underlying problem of insufficient damage output vs Rally scaling.
Run 73 analysis: 4th Spheric Guardian death, Artifact stripping, Demon Form too slow

playbook/cards/uppercut.md
@@ -1,8 +1,8 @@
-# Uppercut (2E, Attack, 13 damage + 1 Weak + 1 Vulnerable)
-Applies BOTH major debuffs in one card.
+# Uppercut (2E, Attack, 13 damage + 1 Weak + 1 Vulnerable | +: 2 Weak + 2 Vulnerable)
+Applies BOTH major debuffs in one card. The best single-card Artifact stripper in the Ironclad pool.
-WHEN IT'S GOOD: Against single targets where both debuffs matter. Against Artifact enemies (strips 2 charges).
-DECISION POINTS: At 2E, is dual-debuff worth the cost? Against Spheric Guardian (3 Artifact), Uppercut strips 2 charges efficiently. Against Gremlin Nob, it's safe (Attack card).
-UPGRADE: Likely extends debuff duration to 2 turns each.
-SYNERGIES: Artifact stripping (each debuff consumes 1 charge separately).
-MATCHUPS: Spheric Guardian -- strips 2 of 3 Artifact charges in one play.
+WHEN IT'S GOOD: Against single targets where both debuffs matter. Against Artifact enemies (strips 2 charges). Upgraded version applies 2 turns of each debuff, making it the most efficient debuff card at 2E.
+DECISION POINTS: At 2E, is dual-debuff worth the cost? Against Spheric Guardian (3 Artifact), Uppercut strips 2 charges efficiently. Against Gremlin Nob, it's safe (Attack card). Upgraded Uppercut is high priority against any Artifact enemy.
+UPGRADE: Extends debuff duration to 2 turns each (confirmed). Significant -- doubles debuff value and makes the 2E cost worthwhile in longer fights.
+SYNERGIES: Artifact stripping (each debuff consumes 1 charge separately). Spot Weakness (Vulnerable from Uppercut amplifies Strength-boosted attacks). Demon Form (sustained Vulnerability keeps damage high as Strength ramps).
+MATCHUPS: Spheric Guardian -- strips 2 of 3 Artifact charges in one play. Confirmed effective in Run 75: Uppercut+ stripped Artifact charges, enabling subsequent debuffs. However, even with successful Artifact stripping, Spheric Guardian's Barricade block can still be insurmountable if damage scaling is too slow.
playbook/enemies/spheric-guardian.md
@@ -3,9 +3,15 @@ PATTERN: Starts with 40 block + Barricade (block persists) + 3 Artifact + applie
KEY MECHANIC: Barricade means block never expires. The Spheric Guardian GAINS block on defend turns (observed 15-20 block gained per action), and since block persists via Barricade, it stacks indefinitely. Combined with alternating attack/defend, the block grows faster than chip damage can remove it. At low Strength (0-1), Strikes deal 6-7 per play vs 15-20 block gained per defend cycle. The block will grow every other turn even while being attacked on the off turns.
WHAT THIS MEANS: This is a DPS check disguised as a hallway fight. At 1 Strength, Strikes deal 6-7 damage per play. The Spheric Guardian gains 15-20 block on defend turns and retains leftover block. After 5-6 turns, its block reaches 40-64 and never comes down. The fight becomes mathematically unwinnable without sufficient Strength scaling or burst damage. Strip 3 Artifact charges first with cheap debuffs (Thunderclap ideal). Then apply Vulnerable. Then burst through remaining block + HP.
BENCHMARKS: Must kill within 6-8 turns or block becomes insurmountable. Expect 30-50+ HP lost from Frail-reduced blocking vs 10x2 attacks. At low Strength without burst, this fight is a guaranteed loss through attrition.
-BRING: Thunderclap (strip Artifact), Pen Nib (double damage to break block), burst damage (Fiend Fire, Immolate, Bludgeon), Strength scaling (Inflame, Spot Weakness), Headbutt (find specific cards).
-AVOID: Chip damage with Strikes (block regenerates faster than you can deplete it at low Strength). Havoc (random exhaust can destroy key cards in a long DPS race). Random exhaust effects in general. Exhausting your own key cards with Second Wind (observed: Second Wind exhausted Shockwave+, True Grit+, and block cards, sealing a loss). Playing Brutality (1 HP/turn self-damage over an 8-13 turn fight is 8-13 HP lost -- confirmed to cause death in this fight). Playing Rage+ expecting persistent block (Rage expires at end of turn, does NOT carry over). Exhausting Spot Weakness (permanent Strength gain is one of the few ways to break through Barricade block). Entering at low HP (<50%) -- this fight routinely drains 30-50 HP, and even full HP entry resulted in death.
+BRING: Uppercut+ (strips 2 Artifact charges in one play, best single-card Artifact solution), Thunderclap (strip remaining Artifact), Pen Nib (double damage to break block), burst damage (Fiend Fire, Immolate, Bludgeon), FAST Strength scaling (Inflame, Spot Weakness, Limit Break -- NOT Demon Form), Headbutt (find specific cards), potions (Strength Potion, Fire Potion -- any supplemental damage matters).
+AVOID: Chip damage with Strikes (block regenerates faster than you can deplete it at low Strength). Demon Form as sole Strength source (too slow -- 13 Str by turn 9 still lost to 65+ block). Havoc (random exhaust can destroy key cards in a long DPS race). Random exhaust effects in general. Exhausting your own key cards with Second Wind (observed: Second Wind exhausted Shockwave+, True Grit+, and block cards, sealing a loss). Playing Brutality (1 HP/turn self-damage over an 8-13 turn fight is 8-13 HP lost -- confirmed to cause death in this fight). Playing Rage+ expecting persistent block (Rage expires at end of turn, does NOT carry over). Exhausting Spot Weakness (permanent Strength gain is one of the few ways to break through Barricade block). Entering at low HP (<50%) -- this fight routinely drains 30-50 HP, and even full HP entry resulted in death. Entering with zero potions -- potions are the only way to supplement insufficient scaling in this fight.
APPEARS WITH: Sentry (in Act 2 hallway fights). When paired, incoming damage is 9 (Sentry) + 10x2 (Spheric Guardian) = 29 per turn. Frail from Spheric Guardian reduces block by 25%, making Defend give only 3 instead of 5. At low HP, the combined damage is often unblockable. Kill the Sentry first (no block, lower HP) to reduce incoming damage and stop Dazed generation. Then focus Spheric Guardian with burst damage. Metallicize's passive 3-4 block/turn helps but is insufficient alone against 20 incoming.
-WARNING: This is the single most dangerous Act 2 hallway fight. Three deaths confirmed against this enemy (at 26%, 43%, and 100% HP entry). The third death occurred at FULL HP (87/87) in a Sentry + Spheric Guardian pairing from an Unknown room. Despite having Barricade (player card), Rage+, Rampage+, Blood for Blood, and Stone Calendar, the fight was still lost because: (1) Brutality's 1 HP/turn self-damage ticked HP from 9 to 1 over the long fight, making survival impossible, (2) Rage+ expires at end of turn (not permanent), so the player had no block generation on the critical final turn, and (3) Frail 5 reduced block cards by 25%, making even True Grit+ insufficient. Even entering at full HP, the fight drained 86 HP over 6 turns through a combination of Spheric Guardian attacks, Sentry attacks, and Brutality self-damage. Reaper is useless in this fight (all damage absorbed by block = zero healing). Brutality is actively harmful (extends the death clock). Only massive single-turn burst or sustained Strength scaling can win.
+ARTIFACT STRIPPING: Spheric Guardian starts with 3 Artifact charges. Uppercut+ is the best single-card Artifact stripper -- it applies Weak 2 and Vulnerable 2, consuming 2 Artifact charges in one play. Follow with any single debuff (Bash, Thunderclap) to strip the 3rd. Once Artifact is gone, Vulnerable massively increases kill speed through the Barricade block.
+
+FRAIL 5 INTERACTION: Frail 5 reduces block from ALL sources by 25%. Defend gives 3 instead of 5. Shrug It Off gives 6 instead of 8. This lasts 5 turns and cannot be removed while Artifact charges remain. Combined with 10x2 attacks, the effective damage per attack turn is ~24+ (20 damage minus reduced block). This is the primary reason low-Strength decks lose -- block is insufficient AND damage is insufficient simultaneously.
+
+DEMON FORM SCALING: Even Demon Form scaling to 13 Strength by turn 9 was insufficient to kill Spheric Guardian through 65+ Barricade block. The fight lasted 9 turns and still ended in a loss. Demon Form is too slow -- by the time Strength ramps, block is already insurmountable. Front-loaded Strength (Spot Weakness, Inflame, Limit Break) or burst damage is required.
+
+WARNING: This is the single most dangerous Act 2 hallway fight. Four deaths confirmed against this enemy (at 26%, 43%, 47%, and 100% HP entry). The fourth death (47% HP, 38/80) involved Demon Form + Metallicize + Spot Weakness scaling that reached 13 Str by turn 9, with Uppercut+ stripping Artifact charges -- and still lost. Zero potions available. Frail 5 crippled blocking during the critical early turns while Demon Form was ramping. Barricade block reached 65+ and grew faster than 13 Str could deplete it. The third death occurred at FULL HP (87/87) in a Sentry + Spheric Guardian pairing from an Unknown room. Despite having Barricade (player card), Rage+, Rampage+, Blood for Blood, and Stone Calendar, the fight was still lost because: (1) Brutality's 1 HP/turn self-damage ticked HP from 9 to 1 over the long fight, making survival impossible, (2) Rage+ expires at end of turn (not permanent), so the player had no block generation on the critical final turn, and (3) Frail 5 reduced block cards by 25%, making even True Grit+ insufficient. Even entering at full HP, the fight drained 86 HP over 6 turns through a combination of Spheric Guardian attacks, Sentry attacks, and Brutality self-damage. Reaper is useless in this fight (all damage absorbed by block = zero healing). Brutality is actively harmful (extends the death clock). Potions are critical for this fight -- entering with zero potions removes the only way to supplement insufficient scaling. Only massive single-turn burst or sustained Strength scaling that comes ONLINE FAST (Inflame, Spot Weakness, not Demon Form) can win.
Run 72 analysis: Gremlin Leader Rally, Masked Bandits, shop bug documented
55c7ac8d
2026-05-07
playbook/cards/havoc.md
@@ -16,3 +16,5 @@ SYNERGIES: Headbutt (place a specific card on top, then Havoc plays it for free
ANTI-SYNERGY: Any deck with irreplaceable scaling cards (Rampage, Fiend Fire, Feed). The random exhaust risk outweighs the 0-energy benefit. DO NOT take Havoc if the deck depends on specific cards surviving to future fights.
MATCHUPS: Gremlin Nob -- Havoc is a Skill, triggers Enrage. Do NOT play.
+
+SHOP BUG WARNING: In one run, a shop index mismatch caused 2 copies of Havoc to be purchased instead of the intended Inflame. This is a cmd.py infrastructure bug, not a player decision error, but the result was a deck polluted with 2 unwanted Havocs. If Havoc appears in the deck unexpectedly, treat it as a dead card -- do NOT play it in fights with irreplaceable cards in the draw pile.
playbook/enemies/gremlin-leader.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Gremlin Leader (elite, Act 2, HP: ~131)
PATTERN: Summons Sneaky Gremlins (~10-12 HP each). Uses DEFEND_BUFF (Rally): gives ALL enemies +3 Strength permanently and stacking. Gremlins attack for ~9 base + accumulated Str. Leader itself attacks for significant damage when not rallying. Leader re-summons gremlins when they die -- killing gremlins is temporary relief, not permanent removal.
-KEY MECHANIC: Rally gives +3 Str to ALL enemies (Leader + all gremlins). Permanent and stacking. After 2 rallies: every enemy has +6 Str. The fight's damage scales exponentially with turn count. With +3 Str after one rally, gremlins deal 9+3=12 each. With two gremlins attacking, that is 24 damage from gremlins alone, plus the Leader's own attacks. Confirmed: at 32% HP entry (28/86), Rally + 2 gremlins attacking dealt 36+ combined damage on turn 2, overwhelming 19 block + 10 HP remaining. The fight is unwinnable at low HP because there is no time to set up before Rally scaling makes incoming damage lethal.
-WHAT THIS MEANS: KILL GREMLINS FAST to reduce per-turn damage, but prioritize Leader damage when possible because gremlins get re-summoned. AOE is critical for clearing gremlins efficiently. Apply Shockwave early for mass Weak + Vulnerable. The fight becomes mathematically unwinnable once Strength stacks too high (by turn 8+, incoming damage is 49+).
-BENCHMARKS: Must win by turn 6-7 or exhaust cards run out and scaling overwhelms you. Enter with 60%+ HP minimum. At 25% HP (14/56), the fight is unwinnable even with Nilry's Codex generating Impervious and Immolate.
+KEY MECHANIC: Rally gives +3 Str to ALL enemies (Leader + all gremlins). Permanent and stacking. After 2 rallies: every enemy has +6 Str. The fight's damage scales exponentially with turn count. With +3 Str after one rally, gremlins deal 9+3=12 each. With two gremlins attacking, that is 24 damage from gremlins alone, plus the Leader's own attacks. Second data point: at 54% HP entry (43/80), turn 3 total enemy damage was 44 (mathematically unblockable with a 19-card deck containing 5 Strikes). Even with FNP + Shockwave+ + Fiend Fire+ burst tools, the fight ended in death because gremlins + Leader combined output exceeded all available block. The fight is unwinnable at low HP because there is no time to set up before Rally scaling makes incoming damage lethal.
+WHAT THIS MEANS: KILL GREMLINS FAST to reduce per-turn damage, but prioritize Leader damage when possible because gremlins get re-summoned. AOE is critical for clearing gremlins efficiently. Apply Shockwave early for mass Weak + Vulnerable. The fight becomes mathematically unwinnable once Strength stacks too high (by turn 3-4 with multiple gremlins alive, incoming damage is 40+).
+BENCHMARKS: Must win by turn 5-6 or Rally scaling overwhelms you. Enter with 60%+ HP minimum -- 54% (43/80) was insufficient even with strong burst tools. At 25% HP (14/56), the fight is unwinnable even with Nilry's Codex generating Impervious and Immolate. Bloated decks (19 cards with 5 Strikes) cannot draw answers fast enough against Rally's exponential scaling.
BRING: AOE (Thunderclap, Whirlwind, Immolate+), Shockwave+ (mass debuff), Disarm (reduce Leader Str). Non-exhaust damage cards for sustained fighting. Rage (0E power that generates block per attack -- excellent value in multi-attack turns). Potions -- this fight demands aggressive potion use.
AVOID: Entering below 60% HP. Exhaust-heavy decks (once Impervious/Shockwave/Disarm are gone, remaining deck is too weak). If below 50% HP, take any alternative path. Decks with reduced Max HP (e.g., post-Vampires at 56 Max HP) have no margin for error against this fight's scaling.

playbook/events/masked-bandits.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Masked Bandits (Act 2 Event)
+
+OPTIONS:
+1. **Pay gold (toll)** — Lose all gold, avoid combat. The safe option when at low HP.
+2. **Fight** — Fight a group of bandits. Standard combat encounter.
+
+WHEN TO PAY: At low HP where combat risks death. Gold is recoverable; HP is not. If below 60% HP and the alternative is a fight that could cascade into a death spiral, pay the toll without hesitation. Gold has no value if you die.
+
+WHEN TO FIGHT: At high HP with a strong deck. The fight is a standard Act 2 hallway encounter. If the deck handles hallway fights cleanly, fight to preserve gold for shops.
+
+STRATEGIC NOTE: This event appeared in Run 72 at critically low HP (4 HP after the Chosen + Byrd fight). Paying the toll was correct -- any combat at 4/80 HP is near-certain death. The toll preserved the run long enough to reach the next rest site.
+
+---

## Uncommon Relics
playbook/relics/eternal-feather.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Eternal Feather
+RARITY: Common
+EFFECT: For every 5 cards in your deck, heal 3 HP after combat.
+
+HOW IT CHANGES DECISIONS: Supplements Burning Blood's flat 6 HP/fight. At 15 cards: +9 HP/fight (total 15 with Burning Blood). At 20 cards: +12 HP/fight (total 18). At 25 cards: +15 HP/fight (total 21). Rewards larger decks, which conflicts with typical deck-thinning strategy. Do not take extra cards just to increase Eternal Feather healing -- deck quality matters more than marginal HP gains.
+
+EVALUATION: Mediocre. The healing scales with deck size, but larger decks draw key cards less reliably. In a typical 15-20 card deck, the healing is modest (9-12 HP). Compare to Meat on the Bone (12 HP if below 50%) or Burning Blood (6 HP flat). Only valuable when the run already has a larger deck and no better relic options.
+
+---
playbook/relics/oddly-smooth-stone.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Oddly Smooth Stone
+RARITY: Common
+EFFECT: Gain 1 Dexterity.
+
+HOW IT CHANGES DECISIONS: Permanent +1 block on every block card played. Defend generates 6 block instead of 5. Shrug It Off generates 9 instead of 8. Compounds with multiple block cards per turn. Over a long fight, this adds up to 10-20 extra block total.
+
+EVALUATION: Solid common relic. Passive defensive boost with no downside. Less impactful than Vajra (+1 Strength) for Ironclad because Ironclad's win condition is typically killing fast, not blocking more. But in Act 2 where chip damage accumulates, the extra block helps reduce HP drain between rest sites.
+
+---
